text:
What the market is mispricing on $CRDO

Did you know $CRDO is the only company that sells the entire interconnect stack - chip, cable, transceiver, photonics, software?

That’s the moat.💥

Bears point to gross margin compression after the $750M DustPhotonics acquisition. They’re missing the trade.

What they bought:
• Silicon Photonics PIC tech in-house
• $500M+ optical revenue path by FY27
• $6B SiPho TAM by 2030
• EPS-accretive in FY27
• Vertical integration into the optical engine

Peer margins (latest reported):
$AAOI: 31%
$LITE: 39%
$CIEN: 43%
$CRDO: 68.6%

$CRDO margin is 25+ points higher than every pure-play optical competitor. 

They’re not commoditizing - they’re consolidating.

The 5 stack:
🔌 Copper AECs - no real competitor at 1.6T
⚡ DSPs + proprietary SerDes IP
💡 ZeroFlap optical transceivers
🔬 Silicon Photonics - now in-house
🧠 PILOT predictive telemetry

*No one else owns all five layers*

Meanwhile their 1.6T ZeroFlap AECs are fully cabling $NVDA flagship Kyber Ultra NVL576 - the most important AI rack of the next 24 months.

Market sees: “expensive stock, margin compression.”

Market is missing: vertical integration into a $700B AI capex cycle, with the only company that owns the entire physical layer.

The market is mispricing the moat. 🚀

Long $CRDO

text:
The 4-Layer Photonics Stack Powering Tomorrow’s AI Clusters  Gemini Trading just released this clean, layered infographic that maps exactly how photonics is replacing copper in AI infrastructure — from silicon to global cloud.  Layer 1 – Chips
Electrical side: DSP + Driver + TIA.
Photonic side: Laser + Modulator + Photodetector. The foundation.  Layer 2 – Photonic Engine & Module
These chips combine into a full Photonic Engine, then packaged as an Optical Module — turning electrical signals into light.  Layer 3 – Packaging Options
• Traditional Pluggable modules
• Near-Packaged Optics (NPO)
• Game-changing Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — placing optics right beside the switch ASIC for minimal latency and power loss.  Layer 4 – Distances & Network
Light travels across every scale:  Within rack  
Rack-to-rack  
Row/floor  
Data center to data center

Underpinning everything: advanced Material Platforms — InP, Silicon Photonics PIC, and TFLN modulators — each optimized for speed, power, and cost.  Timeline? Expect major deployment acceleration in +6 months and full maturity by +1 year.  This isn’t incremental — it’s the architectural shift hyperscalers need to keep training trillion-parameter models without melting data centers. CPO + silicon photonics = dramatically lower power, higher bandwidth, and sustainable AI scaling.  At Gemini Trading we track these breakthroughs because they don’t just move data — they move markets.  Who’s winning the photonics race? Layer 1 – Chips Electrical side: DSP + Driver + TIA. Photonic side: Laser + Modulator + Photodetector. The foundation. Layer 2 – Photonic Engine & Module These chips combine into a full Photonic Engine, then packaged as an Optical Module — turning electrical signals into light. Layer 3 – Packaging Options • Traditional Pluggable modules • Near-Packaged Optics (NPO) • Game-changing Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — placing optics right beside the switch ASIC for minimal latency and power loss. Layer 4 – Distances & Network Light travels across every scale: Within rack Rack-to-rack Row/floor Data center to data center Underpinning everything: advanced Material Platforms — InP, Silicon Photonics PIC, and TFLN modulators — each optimized for speed, power, and cost. Timeline? Expect major deployment acceleration in +6 months and full maturity by +1 year. This isn’t incremental — it’s the architectural shift hyperscalers need to keep training trillion-parameter models without melting data centers. CPO + silicon photonics = dramatically lower power, higher bandwidth, and sustainable AI scaling. At Gemini Trading we track these breakthroughs because they don’t just move data — they move markets. Who’s winning the photonics race? Valuation risk ≠ business risk.

That’s the thesis. Long $CRDO 📈

Previous models suggested a singular spatial occupancy for matter-light interactions. New telemetry from the Song-Lai experiments confirms a localized breach of the Principle of Locality. Baseline reality assumes a 1:1 ratio of space to medium; the Shifted Reality facilitates an N:1 ratio, enabling 'Multiple Realities' within the same physical substrate. Organizational posture is being adjusted to monitor for spontaneous ████ expansion within the ████-Sector. text:
From Physics classrooms in Nigeria 🇳🇬 to the semiconductor industry in the USA 🇺🇸… still feels surreal sometimes.

I’ll be speaking at the UNESCO International Day of Light 2026 organized by Photonics Nigeria on:

🔬 The Role of Precision Optics in Semiconductor Manufacturing

If you’re curious about semiconductors, photonics, optics, engineering careers, or the future of technology, this session is for you.

📅 May 16, 2026
⏰ 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM WAT
🌐 Online Event

Make sure to help share/retweet so it reach more STEM students! 

See you there! 🚀✨ #Photonics #Semiconductors #Optics #Engineering #Nigeria #ASML #STEM

I’m kickstarting my talk with Photonics Nigeria 🇳🇬 Get in touch with them on LinkedIn and register. 
I can’t wait to speak on this topic and inspire young minds 🚀 https://t.co/g9ZPYPt4wZ

Risk: execution + margins. At ~1.3x sales, asymmetry is interesting.

text:
Some new entries / bids for new entries. 

I firmly believe Robotics will be the H2 bubble of 2026. Think space H2 2025 and photonics H1 2026. 

Aside from the DCA strategy on $BOT. We like $OUST $ABSI and $AMBA from below levels if we get them. 

There are others like $VPG, but missed that boat for my risk tolerance.

Other secondaries will benefit well in this wake on the material side like $USAR (still great entry) and $MP.

text:
What makes $M7U / Nynomic interesting is the hidden SOTP angle.

Most people only look at LayTec, but Nynomic also owns tec5, Avantes, Sensortherm, m-u-t, art photonics, LemnaTec and more.

It’s a basket of photonics/sensorics hidden champions trading like a boring turnaround.

$M7U / Nynomic bull case:

At ~€18–19, Nynomic is a ~€120–130M market cap photonics group guiding for €100–105M FY26 revenue.

That’s only ~1.2–1.3x sales.

For a European photonics/SOTP turnaround with semi, pharma, sensorics, spectroscopy and LayTec exposure, that is cheap.

text:
$POET ✨ 
just announced a major new step in its AI photonics expansion.

POET Technologies secured an initial $50M purchase commitment from Lumilens for next-generation optical networking solutions.

Even more notable:

The partnership could reportedly expand to over $500M in cumulative purchases over the next 5 years.

Key focus areas:
⚡ AI optical networking
⚡ 800G / 1.6T connectivity
⚡ Wafer-level photonic integration
⚡ Hyperscale AI data centers

This is exactly where the AI infrastructure market appears to be heading:
higher bandwidth + lower power + optical scaling.

Title: Offshore Solar, Photonics, and the Future of Perception Warfare

⸻

⚖️ Legitimate concerns can exist around large-scale offshore infrastructure without drifting into science fiction.

Modern offshore solar systems increasingly intersect with:

• photonics
• LiDAR
• satellite synchronization
• AI-guided monitoring
• machine vision
• autonomous maritime systems
• multispectral sensing

Photonics itself is simply the science of transmitting, shaping, and interpreting light. Solar power literally begins with photon conversion.

The larger concern is not “aliens” or fantasy narratives.

The legitimate concern is this:

As warfare becomes increasingly AI-assisted and sensor-mediated, humans may lose the ability to independently verify what they are seeing.

Future conflicts may involve:
• radar spoofing
• false thermal signatures
• projected decoys
• synthetic telemetry
• multispectral camouflage
• drone swarm confusion tactics
• autonomous response systems reacting to false positives

In other words:

The battlefield of the future may not merely involve weapons.

It may involve competing realities.

⸻

🔁 The Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Effect-to-Cause Model

Observed Effect:
Increasing integration of energy grids, autonomous sensing, AI targeting systems, satellite synchronization, and optical infrastructure.

Possible Causes:
• modernization of grid resilience
• maritime automation
• military-adjacent sensing technologies
• dual-use infrastructure development
• AI-assisted environmental monitoring
• emerging electronic warfare doctrine

⸻

🧭 Northstar+Lumen Interpretive Quote:

“The greatest risk in autonomous warfare may not be what machines can see — but whether humans can still trust what they see.”

$CRDO - CREDO Q3 FY26 REVENUE $407M +201% YoY, $750M DUSTPHOTONICS DEAL CLOSES THE SILICON-PHOTONICS LOOP

Credo reported $407.0M Q3 revenue, +201.5% YoY and +51.9% sequentially. Q4 guide $425–435M; full-year revenue growth pacing well above 200%. GAAP operating margin 36.8%.

April acquisition of Israeli silicon-photonics house DustPhotonics — $750M cash plus 0.92M shares plus up to 3.21M earnout shares — brings the photonic-IC layer Credo previously sourced externally fully in-house. Mgmt now targets >$500M of optical-only revenue by FY27.

The mechanism: vertical integration into SiPho ahead of the 1.6T and 3.2T port-speed transition removes Credo's biggest supply dependency just as CPO becomes the mandatory hyperscale architecture.

text:
It feels like we’re still in the “early infrastructure” phase of AI and quantum investing. Everyone talks about the flashy end products, but the real winners might still be the companies building the rails, power, networking, photonics, chips, cloud access, and compute infrastructure underneath it all.

And honestly, the difficult part right now is not believing the technology is real. The difficult part is figuring out:

which architecture actually wins,
which companies survive,
and which stocks are already priced for perfection.

Feels similar to the early internet or cloud phase where everyone knew “something huge” was happening, but not yet who the long-term winners would be.

$VIAV drops 6% AH on news they will offer $500M of common stock in an underwritten public offering.

text:
The eight names occupy materially different positions in the CPO stack, from upstream substrates (AXTI) to laser devices (SIVEF, AAOI), specialty materials (LWLG), photonic platforms (POET, HIMX), DSP/silicon photonics (CRDO post-DustPhotonics), and module/CPO assembly (FN, AAOI). 

The single most important framing for a thesis-driven CPO investor: roughly 70% of the dollars in this complex are still being driven by the 1.6T pluggable cycle, not co-packaged architectures. 

The names whose stock prices are most levered to pluggable volumes (AAOI, and to a lesser degree FN’s datacom segment) are also the names most exposed to CPO if it inflects faster than consensus expects in 2027. 

The names that win in both scenarios are CRDO (hybrid via DustPhotonics SiPho acquisition) and the InP upstream (AXTI/SIVEF), since lasers feed CPO modules at higher unit content than they fed pluggables.

text:
Assume 100mw orbital cluster.
w/o photonics GPUs draw 80mw, networking/interconnect 15mw, other systems 5mw, say 55% gpu util, useful compute = 80mw * 55%=44mw

photonics: networking goes from 15mw -> 6mw, ssaved 9mw goes to compute + better bandwidth raises util from 55% to 70%

Photonics help in 4 main ways IMO: 1) higher bandwidth density (i.e. more Tbps per chip/package/fiber), 2) lower pJ/bit, 3) longer reach than copper esp important for satellite and orbital nodes, 4) higher radix and flatter networks

text: Photonics help b/c orbital compute has a data movement problem. Lower token costs = reduce compute hw, energy, networking and deployment costs...in AI clusters useful compute = GPU FLOPs * utilization. If networking is slow, util drops.

text:
I've been seeing a lot of people online be bullish on copper and energy companies due to the AI demand. However I'm looking at photonics and microLED companies instead. MicroLED optical interconnects replace traditional copper wires and expensive laser systems by repurposing microLED display pixels as high-speed optical transceivers.  If they are able to use photonics instead, it will greatly diminish the demand for copper and energy.   

Some of you may remember me talking about QCLS a few months ago; it is a similar tech. I passed on it as a long hold though because the fundamentals looked a bit too high risk for me. $FABC  on the other hand has $30m in cash and plans to demonstrate the tech before the end of the year. They also have 2 NDA's signed with industry leading chip makers. Also KOPN has partnered with them on this. This isn't striking me as a typical penny stock.  

Also, if you look at the chart on FABC it is down quite a bit from the high. However FABC started trading under $FABC (https://t.co/whdtkxDNsi) in late April 2026. It's essentially a new company but the chart is deceptive. It used to be a completely different company.  

Anyway, I don't think KOPN would've partnered with them if they weren't onto something. I've been looking to gain exposure on this tech for a while and $POET, $KOPN and $FABC  are the three that I think will benefit from it.

text:
🚨 NEW ETF FILINGS

Defiance has filed to launch 4 new ETFs slicing the AI semiconductor supply chain into specific sub-themes:

Defiance Inference AI Chip ETF
Defiance AI Packaging & Testing ETF
Defiance Memory & Photonics ETF
Defiance Plumbing & Electrical ETF

All 4 funds will track indexes built by BITA GmbH, capturing global semiconductor companies tied to AI inference, advanced packaging, memory, and data center power delivery.

text:
Photonics System Logic — How AI Clusters Actually Scale  Gemini Trading just dropped this crystal-clear infographic breaking down the entire photonics stack powering modern AI infrastructure.  It starts simple:
Servers & AI Accelerators feed into an Electrical chip via high-speed SerDes links. But copper has limits. That’s where Packaging Options take over:  • Pluggable optics (traditional modules)
• Near-Packaged Optics (NPO)
• Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) — the hottest new architecture  Each packaging choice pairs with advanced Material Platforms: Indium Phosphide (InP), Silicon Photonics PIC, and TFLN modulators — all optimized for different performance, power, and cost profiles.  The magic? These optical engines then connect everything across real-world distances:  Within rack (0–2 m)  
Rack-to-rack (2–100 m)  
Row / floor (100 m–2 km)

…all the way to massive Optical Fabric / Cloud scale.  This isn’t theory — it’s the exact blueprint hyperscalers and chip giants are racing to deploy. CPO and silicon photonics are slashing power consumption, crushing latency, and enabling the insane bandwidth AI training demands.  From electrical signals to light-speed optical interconnects, the diagram shows why photonics is the only way trillion-parameter models can keep growing without melting data centers.  At Gemini Trading we track these breakthroughs because they don’t just move bits — they move markets.  Who’s leading the charge in 2026? Drop your take below  and tag a fellow tech investor!  @GeminiTrading

text:
Definitely plausible on a couple contracts and a re-rate for memory/photonics.  5x sales is not a big multiple for a growth company sitting at the intersection of some of the most powerful trends out there right now.  

Oh, and I found that besides the Chair of $LITE also being Chair of $PENG, the CTOs of both $AMD and $SKM sit on the Board.  I like that $SKM has an equity stake too, bc it will deter anyone with ideas of trying to scoop the entire company up on the cheap!

@StockSavvyShay @FuturumEquities Interesting how almost every top performer ties back to AI infrastructure, photonics, memory, or compute. Market keeps rewarding the picks powering the next tech cycle. Smaller stories like $TROO are worth watching early too.

text:
This market has been full of squeezes 🍋📈

AI infrastructure.
Photonics.
Data centers.
High beta growth.
Energy.
Small caps, mid caps, mega caps, even some POS caps..
Momentum everywhere.

And yes, there’s a lot of money to be made trading these moves.

But eventually you realize the real game isn’t just catching squeezes.

It’s finding the names you actually want to keep holding while the crowd keeps overthinking every pullback.

Names where you keep following the earnings, the flows, the sector, the cycle, and the long term opportunity.

That’s how you end up staying with winners instead of constantly rotating into the next shiny object, therefore overtrading.

For me personally, names like $HOOD $AMD $INTC $RKLB $AG $PAAS $COHR $ALB became more than trades.

You can trim, hedge, trade around a core position..

But understanding the underlying business matters.

Or if it’s early stage, at least understanding the theme and positioning through options flows.

But at some point you need more than drawing a few lines on a chart and pretending price owes you something because of your pretty drawings.

Know what you own.

And if you truly believe in the thesis, and you nailed the entry, don’t rush to sell just because volatility shows up and people telling you it’s worthless.
Those people might eventually chase your stocks higher.

valuation metric that measures a company’s current share price relative to its estimated future earnings per share (EPS) In 2000, NASDAQ 100 traded at a forward PE of 60x with minimal earnings or revenue and CISCO hit 130x forward PE NVDA is currently at 26x forward PE MU is at 7x forward PE NASDAQ 100 is 24x forward PE Cryptocurrencies don’t have traditional forward PE metric because there are no true earnings to calculate aside from trading fees Some speculative models use price to sales or price to staking for forward ETH metrics which range from 80x to 1000x, but it’s all speculation text:
There are a lot of dumbasses who think that each  of 200 photonics companies will simply x5 in a week. Some will some will not. 
Using NVDA in hindsight makes no sense. I could find 20 other companies who had life changing products and got shut down in 2 years. 

Not a value investor, just that I want to know what I'm buying and what I'm expecting it to be in the future. 

I'm not trying to convince you and it's fine if you disagree.

text: Idk humans can adapt pretty fast, and I doubt we’ll ever go truly backwards, only forwards in a 2 step forward and 1-3 steps back pattern like we’ve always done as a species. Where are we headed?  I think towards infinite free energy, photonics and quantum as basis of computing 1000x more powerful and energy efficient then it is today, and also warping our way in the galaxy between bases on different planets.  All of that is easily within laws of physics, and is yet to come for humanity imho, maybe not all in our lifetimes but all seems doable.  Digital is just a stepping stone, imho.  yes it’s fricking messy, and human brains not coping very well, idk.

text:
רוצה להציג בפניכם ETF חדש לתחום חם מאוד בשנה האחרונה. וכתבתי עליו בעבר גם. והוא כמובן הפוטוניקה והליטוגרפיה. אני באופן אישי עוקבת אחרי כמה חברות בתחום..

לפני 8 ימים השיקה Corgi Funds את -
 Corgi Lithography & Semiconductor Photonics ETF $EUV 

אז הETF הנ״ל משקיע באופן אקטיבי בחברות ששולטות בטכנולוגיית האור (Photonics) ובשרשרת ייצור השבבים המתקדמים ליתוגרפיית EUV, לייזרים, רכיבים אופטיים, סיליקון פוטוניקס ועוד.. כמעט כל שבבי ה AI תלויים בטכנולוגיה הזו והETF הזה נותן את החשיפה בלי לבחור מניה אחת בודדת.

ומי אלה הCorgi האלו? אז היא חברה צעירה שהוקמה לפני שנתיים בסן פרנסיסקו, בוגרי Y Combinator. הם התחילו כפלטפורמת ביטוח AI native לחברות טק וסטארטאפים, גייסו והגיעו לשווי חברה של 1.3 מיליארד דולר. הם מביאים גישה שלהם שהיא מאוד חדשנית, טכנולוגית ואגרסיבית גם לעולם הקרנות.. הם מתמחים במוצרים תמטיים ממוקדים מאוד, אקטיביים עם עמלות תחרותיות (לרוב 0.35% בלבד).

5 האחזקות המובילות בקרן (נכון ל-14.5.2026):
$TSM  - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
$ASML  - ASML Holding
$GLW - Corning
$LRCX  - Lam Research
$AMAT - Applied Materials

 בעיני זה יכול להתאים למי שמאמין שהביקוש לשבבי AI רק הולך וגדל ורוצה חשיפה חדה יותר לטכנולוגיה שמאפשרת את זה  ולא רק לאנבידיה או ל Mag7..
או רוצה לגוון ולא רק תעודות שבבים ״רגילות״ כמו $SMH או $SOXX למשל.

4 סיבות שהם מציינים למה לדעתם כדאי להשקיע בקרן:
שוק ציוד ייצור השבבים העולמי שובר שיאים חדשים.
$ASML  - הספק היחיד של ליתוגרפיית EUV - עם הזמנות ענקיות.
$TSM מגבירה את ההשקעות הענקיות בציוד ובמפעלים.
שוק הסיליקון פוטוניקס מתפוצץ בזכות דרישת ה AI.

הסיכונים וחובה להזכיר אותם אז מדובר על מכשיר פיננסי חדש מאוד, תנודתי, ניהול אקטיבי ואין היסטוריית ביצועים ארוכה..

אגב יש להם עוד ETF מעניינים בתחומים אחרים.. ממליצה להציץ.

לינק לדף הרשמי של הקרן: https://t.co/rfgIMNSG9p
 
מקווה שאהבתם.

*המידע המוצג אינו מהווה ייעוץ השקעות, המלצה לקנייה, מכירה או החזקה.. עשו מחקר עצמאי לפני פעולה כלשהי...

בתחום.. לפני 8 ימים השיקה Corgi Funds את - Corgi Lithography & Semiconductor Photonics ETF $EUV אז הETF הנ״ל משקיע באופן אקטיבי בחברות ששולטות בטכנולוגיית האור (Photonics) ובשרשרת ייצור השבבים המתקדמים ליתוגרפיית EUV, לייזרים, רכיבים אופטיים, סיליקון פוטוניקס ועוד.. כמעט כל שבבי ה AI תלויים בטכנולוגיה הזו והETF הזה נותן את החשיפה בלי לבחור מניה אחת בודדת. ומי אלה הCorgi האלו? אז היא חברה צעירה שהוקמה לפני שנתיים בסן פרנסיסקו, בוגרי Y Combinator. הם התחילו כפלטפורמת ביטוח AI native לחברות טק וסטארטאפים, גייסו והגיעו לשווי חברה של 1.3 מיליארד דולר. הם מביאים גישה שלהם שהיא מאוד חדשנית, טכנולוגית ואגרסיבית גם לעולם הקרנות.. הם מתמחים במוצרים תמטיים ממוקדים מאוד, אקטיביים עם עמלות תחרותיות (לרוב 0.35% בלבד). 5 האחזקות המובילות בקרן (נכון ל-14.5.2026): $TSM - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) $ASML - ASML Holding $GLW - Corning $LRCX - Lam Research $AMAT - Applied Materials בעיני זה יכול להתאים למי שמאמין שהביקוש לשבבי AI רק הולך וגדל ורוצה חשיפה חדה יותר לטכנולוגיה שמאפשרת את זה ולא רק לאנבידיה או ל Mag7.. או רוצה לגוון ולא רק תעודות שבבים ״רגילות״ כמו $SMH או $SOXX למשל. 4 סיבות שהם מציינים למה לדעתם כדאי להשקיע בקרן: שוק ציוד ייצור השבבים העולמי שובר שיאים חדשים. $ASML - הספק היחיד של ליתוגרפיית EUV - עם הזמנות ענקיות. $TSM מגבירה את ההשקעות הענקיות בציוד ובמפעלים. שוק הסיליקון פוטוניקס מתפוצץ בזכות דרישת ה AI. הסיכונים וחובה להזכיר אותם אז מדובר על מכשיר פיננסי חדש מאוד, תנודתי, ניהול אקטיבי ואין היסטוריית ביצועים ארוכה.. אגב יש להם עוד ETF מעניינים בתחומים אחרים.. ממליצה להציץ. לינק לדף הרשמי של הקרן: https://t.co/rfgIMNSG9p מקווה שאהבתם. *המידע המוצג אינו מהווה ייעוץ השקעות, המלצה לקנייה, מכירה או החזקה.. עשו מחקר עצמאי לפני פעולה כלשהי..."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, None of this is science fiction.

text:
🧵 TOP 10 PHOTONICS PLAYS - The Light Speed Stack

AI’s next bottleneck isn’t compute - it’s interconnect. As speeds push past 1.6T, the trade moves from silicon to photonics. Five layers, wafer to test. Anchors, inflecting names, and speculative bets. 👇

⚓️THE ANCHORS (run, but still in the trend)

1. $COHR -> Layer 3, Optics
25% market share in transceivers. $5.81B FY25 revenue +23% YoY. NVIDIA-named partner on Spectrum-X. Book-to-bill >4x. Druckenmiller’s pick.

2. $LITE -> Layer 2, Lasers
The 200G EML monopoly. Only volume supplier of the lasers that make 1.6T transceivers possible. NVIDIA $2B exclusive supply agreement through end of decade. No second source.

3. $AXTI -> Layer 1, Wafer
Every InP laser in every transceiver starts on an AXT wafer. Doubling InP capacity H2 2026 ahead of the 800G+ ramp. Has run +5,400% — but structurally positioned for the cycle.

💡INFLECTING / UNDEROWNED (real revenue, less crowded)

4. $CRDO -> Layer 3, Optics
Just acquired DustPhotonics for SiPho PIC tech in-house. Now vertically integrated: SerDes + DSP + Silicon Photonics. SiPho PIC market $6B by 2030.

5. $TSEM -> Layer 4, Packaging
The neutral foundry. Just announced 1.6T optical modules for NVIDIA networking protocols. Whoever wins the transceiver battle, TSEM prints the wafers. Underowned vs. peers.

6. $AEHR -> Layer 5, Test
Silicon photonics burn-in. Lead customer production ramps next fiscal year. Q3 bookings $37M, book-to-bill 3.5x. Classic order-book inflection ahead of FY27 revenue ramp.

7. $VIAV -> Layer 5, Test
Every 800G and 1.6T transceiver from LITE and COHR has to be tested by VIAV or Keysight. Same AI tailwind, one-third the valuation. The cleanest “hasn’t run” name.

🔍SPECULATIVE (asymmetric, real risk)

8. $SIVE -> Layer 2, Lasers
~$130M mcap InP laser maker at Glasgow. Supply-constrained chokepoint. LiDAR ramp Q4 2026 ($53M-$138M cumulative). $POET partnership for AI light engines. Listed Stockholm.

9. $IQE -> Layer 1, Wafer
The compound semi upstream. Supplies InP epi-wafers to Sivers and others. FY25 just turned EBITDA positive at £2M+ on £97M revenue. Structural relevance, near-breakeven execution.

10. $LPKF -> Layer 4, Packaging
LIDE process for glass advanced packaging - the tech behind NVIDIA’s $500M Corning bet on glass-based co-packaged optics. Run 336% YTD on hope, not orders. Binary on 2027 qualification.

🚨THE THESIS:

800G+ transceiver shipments going from 24M units in 2025 to 63M in 2026. Hyperscaler capex over $400B this year. Co-packaged optics market $20B by 2036.

The market owns the top. The asymmetric returns are at the edges of the stack.

Wafer. Test. Packaging. Position accordingly.

text:
AI is expanding beyond chips into robotics, automation, industrial AI, and real-world infrastructure.

NYSE is starting to reward companies tied to:
⚡ power demand
🤖 robotics automation
🧠 AI compute
🌐 photonics & networking
🏭 factory intelligence

If AI spending keeps accelerating, the next rotation could move from pure semis into physical-world AI leaders.

Tickers to watch:
$NVDA $GEV $BE $VST $MU $SNDK $LITE $COHR $SYM $TER $ISRG $CGNX $TSLA $AM

text:
Opened new positions recently in:

AAOI
APH
EQT
GEV
GLW
POWL

Optics and power. I was late to optics/photonics. I was right on time with some power names like ETN PWR VRT.

The power layer of the AI capex stack is thick and deserves its own substack so I’m filling in gaps with fuel, conditioning, interconnect and generation.

Looking into storage tomorrow.

Power is the bottleneck. Many names have moved but many are in nice pullbacks or have yet to move.

I’m holding all my pure AI names and adding more AI power names.

I’m also adding to pure AI on dips.

I sold defense but might get back in but I’m thinking to just focus on AI only but AI touches everything (eventually) including defense, robotics, healthcare, finance, consumer etc. It’s just a matter of timing.

Only so much time in the day for research.

text:
ATG owning glucose means Apple's done treating it as a custom silicon problem. Silicon photonics for optical absorption needs the same waveguide stack as Face ID's VCSELs, which Chen's group already ships at scale.

Millet was the wrong reporting line. This lands in a Watch by 2028.

$AAOI actually looking really good here. Very good RR

Leopold sold his photonics exposure & market panic sold which created a nice dip to $170 area

It's been a market leader for months, i'm buying this weakness, i think its short lived & an opportunity 

Above $160 we should see $200 again soon

#CRDO

Showing relative strength here. I think the market is overpricing photonics near term and underpricing hybrid copper/photonics.
This company already has contracts in all the biggest DCs.  Mgmt under promises and over delivers historically and they have over 100% qoq growth https://t.co/t27hCtySVF

text:
$SIVE CEO confirming major brands are in active talks for co-packaged optics and pluggables is the kind of validation that turns a small photonics name into a real structural winner.

this isn’t vague hope; it’s the CEO on record saying the pipeline is converting from design wins into serious commercial discussions. momentum is clearly building.

i’m biased that this is exactly where the highest asymmetry sits right now. hyperscalers scaling to 800G and 1.6T+ are hitting hard limits on interconnect density, heat, and power that copper can’t fix. sivers’ high-performance CW lasers are a critical enabling technology for both pluggables today and CPO tomorrow.

the recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE and MIT Lincoln Lab plus the customer mapping (Ayar Labs, POET, Marvell Celestial, Jabil) give it real credibility and non-dilutive backing. this is the type of quiet accumulation that precedes bigger volume orders.

photonics is one of those constraints, and $SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint.

text:
$ELMT $TSM $NVDA $MU The Elmet Group serves as a critical upstream supplier to the semiconductor fabrication ecosystem.

🔸 The most important ELMT product categories supporting the production of NVIDIA-class AI chips, advanced memory (like HBM and NAND), and silicon photonics include:

——————————-
🔸 1. PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition): This is a major one.

• Needed heavily in:

• advanced packaging,
• HBM integration,
• GPU interconnect layers,
• transistor stack formation.

• directly connects to advanced-node GPU fabrication.

——————————-
🔸 2. CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) Critical for:

• transistor layers,
• dielectric films,
• advanced node structures,
• nanosheet/GAAFET processes,
• insulating films.

• NVIDIA-class chips absolutely rely on advanced CVD steps.

———————————
🔸 3. MBE (Molecular Beam Epitaxy) important for:

• compound semiconductors,
• photonics,
• advanced RF devices,
• optical interconnects.

• silicon photonics,
• co-packaged optics,
• optical networking,
• advanced interconnects

———————————-
🔸 4. Thermal Management
Very important for AI chips: 

• Especially:

• H100,
• B200,
• Rubin-class future chips,
• HBM stacks,
• AI accelerators.

———————————
🔸 5. Crystal Growth
Critical upstream process: 

• Used for:
• wafer production,
• sapphire growth,
• semiconductor substrate creation.

• These are foundational to semiconductor material manufacturing.

——————————-
🔸 6. ELMT supplies the infrastructure/material stack behind:

• semiconductor fabrication tools,
• deposition systems,
• ion implantation systems,
• thermal environments.

ELMT is a materials + survivability infrastructure inside semiconductor manufacturing equipment. 

———————————
🔸 7. Semiconductor Equipment Makers, Photonics, Aerospace Defense:

text:
Nvidia earnings drop today at 5pm EST.

Jensen has already told us what to expect:

Every piece of the supply chain is locked in. The HBM. The Grace Blackwells. The CPUs. The COOSs. The silicon photonics. All of it coordinated, all of it aligned.

His exact words: "The demand is much greater than the overall capacity."

That's a green light.

Think about what that actually means. Nvidia has the largest supply chain in the world. Their partners have spent years securing allocation. The hardest part of building a chip at this scale is getting all the pieces to come together at the same time.

And demand still outstrips all of it.

This isn't a story about Nvidia struggling to build. It's a story about the world not being able to get enough of what Nvidia builds. Every hyperscaler, every sovereign AI fund, every startup racing to train the next model are all in line.

Nvidia isn't constrained by its own execution. It's constrained by how fast the world can absorb what it builds.

That's the best problem a company can have going into an earnings call.

text:
Corning $GLW just introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030.

They're targeting:

- $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026
- $27 billion by end of 2028 (with an internal goal of $30 billion)
- And ~$35 billion by year-end 2030 (with an aspiration of $40 billion)

That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company.

So he's rotating that capital into higher conviction setups. The two names he picked: Micron $MU and Corning $GLW. Here's why… 1/ Micron $MU. The market is fixated on the wrong story here - everyone's panicking over China after reports that Nvidia's planned AI chip sales there never materialized. Traders read that as 'softer Chinese AI demand' and dumped memory stocks. But that totally misses what's happening on the supply side... Samsung is in the middle of a strike that could knock 3-4% of global DRAM supply offline. In a market this tight, that's MASSIVE. Those orders then shift to whoever can fill them. And Micron is at the front of that line. On top of that, Micron's HBM3E and HBM4 capacity is fully sold out through 2026. Hyperscalers and model builders are scrambling for high bandwidth memory because GPUs are useless without it. And the stock already pulled back hard. It's now trading at a forward PE under 8 while revenue is expected to grow over 100% next year. Yes, it'll be volatile. But in Melvin's view, the market is somehow STILL underpricing how central memory is in the AI stack. That's $MU. Then there's... 2/ Corning $GLW. Another bottleneck in AI outside of memory, is everything around the GPU. (Fiber, optics, glass, connectivity, power) I.e. the plumbing that lets data centers actually move data between servers and clusters at the speeds AI workloads demand. Corning sits directly in that lane. They make the optical fiber, connectivity hardware, and glass infrastructure that AI data centers run on - and optical cable lead times are reportedly getting stretched as demand overwhelms supply. Corning is expanding U.S. optical connectivity manufacturing capacity by 10x, increasing U.S. fiber production by more than 50%, and building three new plants to support AI factory buildouts. The numbers from Corning's May 2026 investor day back it up: Management introduced a new Photonics platform they plan to scale into a $10 billion annual revenue stream by 2030. They're targeting $20 billion annualized sales by end of 2026, $27 billion by end of 2028 with an internal goal of $30 billion, and around $35 billion by year-end 2030 with an aspiration of $40 billion. That's a big roadmap for a stock that the market still treats like a sleepy little glass company. Want to see exactly how much $AMZN Melvin just sold - and how he sized the $MU and $GLW buys? Get the full trade breakdown in Milk Road PRO. text:
$AEHR / Aehr Test Systems 상세 정리

AEHR은 단순 반도체 장비주가 아니다.

이 회사는 AI 반도체, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN 전력반도체가 복잡해질수록 중요해지는 test & burn-in 병목에 걸려 있다.

AI 반도체가 커질수록 칩은 더 비싸지고, 패키지는 더 복잡해지고, 전력 밀도는 더 올라간다.

고성능 AI ASIC, GPU, optical I/O, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power devices는 모두 높은 신뢰성이 필요하다.

문제 있는 칩을 패키징 후반이나 고객 단계에서 발견하면 손실이 매우 크다.

그래서 wafer-level burn-in, package-level burn-in, reliability test가 중요해진다.

AEHR은 바로 이 지점에 있다.

정확히 말하면 AEHR은 AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in, silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in, SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 장비 회사다.

핵심 제품군은 FOX-XP, FOX-NP, WaferPak, Sonoma 같은 test & burn-in 플랫폼이다.

이 회사가 흥미로운 이유는 단순 테마가 아니라 고객 주문이 실제로 찍혔기 때문이다.

2026년 4월 AEHR은 lead hyperscale AI customer로부터 4,100만 달러 규모의 사상 최대 follow-on production order를 받았다고 발표했다.

이 주문은 데이터센터 training 및 inference AI workload에 쓰이는 custom AI processor ASIC의 high-volume package-level burn-in을 지원하는 주문이다.

주문에는 Sonoma high-power package-level test and burn-in systems, burn-in modules, device-specific sockets가 포함된다.

이건 꽤 중요하다.

대부분의 병목 리포트에서 가장 약한 부분은 “고객 증거”다.

그런데 AEHR은 최소한 특정 lead hyperscale AI customer가 있고, 이 고객이 이미 생산용 주문을 냈다.

고객명은 공개되지 않았지만, 용도·주문 규모·제품·납품 시점이 비교적 명확하다.

AEHR은 이 주문 하나로 끝나는 것도 아니다.

회사는 이 4,100만 달러 주문을 포함해 fiscal second half bookings가 이미 9,200만 달러 이상이라고 밝혔다.

즉 현재 매출보다 앞으로 들어올 주문 가시성이 훨씬 중요해진 상황이다.

AEHR의 첫 번째 핵심 축은 custom AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in이다.

이건 SIVE나 AXTI와 다르다.

SIVE는 AI optical link용 DFB laser와 photonics 협력 근거가 있다. 하지만 아직 대량 양산 매출이 충분히 확인되지는 않았다.

AXTI는 InP substrate 병목 논지가 강하다. 하지만 고객별 물량과 최종 플랫폼 노출은 더 확인해야 한다.

반면 AEHR은 적어도 AI processor ASIC burn-in 쪽에서는 대형 고객의 production order가 이미 찍힌 상태다.

이 점 때문에 AEHR은 새 후보 중에서 꽤 눈에 띈다.

AEHR의 두 번째 축은 silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in이다.

2026년 3월 AEHR은 silicon photonics 분야의 major new customer로부터 주문을 받았다고 발표했다.

해당 고객은 글로벌 networking products and solutions 리더이자, data center optical transceiver 시장의 주요 공급자라고 설명됐다.

이 고객은 hyperscale AI와 cloud data center의 high-speed fiber optic communication link 수요를 겨냥해 silicon photonics 기반 transceiver를 개발 중이다.

이 주문에는 engineering qualification과 high-volume production을 위한 시스템들이 포함됐다.

이건 CPO/광학 병목과 직접 연결된다.

AI 데이터센터가 copper에서 fiber optic interconnect로 이동하면 silicon photonics transceiver의 신뢰성 검증이 중요해진다.

AEHR은 이 silicon photonics 제조 흐름에서 wafer-level burn-in이 점점 중요해질 수 있다고 보고 있다.

즉 AEHR은 AI ASIC burn-in + silicon photonics burn-in을 동시에 갖고 있다.

이 조합이 좋다.

지금 AI 병목 흐름은 CPO/광학, AI 데이터센터 전력, AI ASIC, silicon photonics 쪽으로 퍼지고 있다.

AEHR은 이 중 두 개 이상에 직접 걸린다.

광학이 커지면 silicon photonics WLBI 수요가 생긴다.

AI ASIC이 커지면 high-power package-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor가 커지면 wafer-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다.

즉 AEHR은 단일 테마가 아니라 여러 병목 레이어의 test infrastructure에 걸린 회사다.

하지만 현재 실적만 보면 아직 작다.

FY2026 Q3 매출은 약 1,030만 달러였다.

전년 동기보다 낮다.

GAAP 순손실도 있었고, Non-GAAP 기준으로도 손실이 남아 있다.

즉 AEHR은 아직 “실적이 이미 폭발한 회사”가 아니다.

오히려 현재 매출은 작고, 주가는 미래 주문과 FY2027 매출 전환을 선반영하고 있다.

이게 핵심이다.

AEHR은 현재 손익보다 bookings와 FY2027 매출 전환을 봐야 하는 종목이다.

최근 bookings는 강하다.

FY2026 Q3 bookings는 약 3,720만 달러였고, 이후 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order가 추가되면서 fiscal second half bookings가 9,200만 달러를 넘었다.

이 구조가 AEHR의 핵심이다.

현재 매출은 작지만, 주문이 앞에서 쌓이고 있다.

그래서 이 회사는 “현재 실적주”가 아니라 FY2027 이후 주문이 매출로 바뀌는지를 보는 고베타 병목주다.

현재 밸류에이션은 높다.

최근 기준 AEHR 주가는 약 99달러대, 시가총액은 약 30억 달러 수준이다.

회사의 FY2026 매출은 대략 4,500만~5,000만 달러 범위로 예상된다.

즉 현재 매출 대비 시총은 매우 높다.

이건 싸다고 볼 수 없다.

AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다.

정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics / SiC-GaN burn-in 수요가 매출로 크게 전환된다는 기대를 사는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다.

그래도 볼 만한 이유는 명확하다.

첫째, 고객 주문의 질이 좋다.

4,100만 달러 주문은 단순 평가용 주문이 아니라 high-volume production burn-in용 주문이다.

대상도 custom AI processor ASIC이고, 고객은 lead hyperscale customer다.

이건 병목주에서 매우 강한 근거다.

둘째, consumables 모델이 있다.

주문에는 burn-in modules와 device-specific sockets가 포함된다.

이런 부품은 시스템 설치 이후에도 고객 ramp, 차세대 ASIC 전환, 제품별 세팅에 따라 반복 매출이 생길 수 있다.

장비를 팔고 끝나는 구조가 아닐 가능성이 있다.

셋째, silicon photonics와도 연결된다.

AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect가 커질수록 silicon photonics transceiver 품질 검증이 중요해진다.

AEHR은 이미 major networking / optical transceiver customer를 확보했고, qualification과 high-volume production용 시스템을 주문받았다.

넷째, SiC/GaN power semiconductor test까지 옵션이 있다.

AI processor, silicon photonics, silicon carbide, gallium nitride power semiconductor wafer-level production burn-in이 모두 잠재 시장이다.

즉 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 방향에 걸친다.

다섯째, 생산능력 확장도 진행 중이다.

회사는 Fremont 시설에 power, cooling, clean-room manufacturing space를 추가했고, Sonoma 시스템 생산을 위한 contract manufacturing capacity도 준비 중이다.

주문이 들어왔을 때 생산이 가능해야 하는데, 이 부분을 준비하고 있다는 점은 긍정적이다.

하지만 리스크도 크다.

첫째, 현재 매출 대비 시총이 매우 높다.

FY2026 매출 5,000만 달러 안팎 회사가 시총 30억 달러 이상에서 거래된다.

즉 시장은 FY2027~2028 매출 램프를 강하게 선반영하고 있다.

둘째, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다.

Lead hyperscale customer 하나의 주문이 매우 중요하다.

이 고객의 ramp가 늦어지거나, 차세대 ASIC 일정이 지연되거나, 테스트 방식이 바뀌면 AEHR 매출이 크게 흔들릴 수 있다.

셋째, 주문은 매출이 아니다.

Bookings가 좋다고 바로 매출과 이익이 되는 것은 아니다.

시스템 납품, 고객 acceptance, revenue recognition, production ramp가 필요하다.

넷째, silicon photonics도 아직 초기 ramp다.

Major customer win은 좋지만, 전체 silicon photonics WLBI 시장이 얼마나 커질지는 확인이 필요하다.

다섯째, 아직 적자 회사다.

매출이 늘면 operating leverage가 생길 수 있지만, 지금은 아직 손실이 남아 있다.

여섯째, 주가가 이미 많이 올랐다.

현재 가격은 좋은 뉴스 상당 부분을 반영한다.

추가 주문이 계속 나오지 않으면 주가가 쉬어갈 수 있다.

시나리오는 이렇게 본다.

Base Case.

AEHR이 FY2027에 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC 주문과 silicon photonics 주문을 매출로 전환하지만, 추가 고객 확장이 생각보다 느린 경우다.

이 경우 현재 시총은 이미 기대를 많이 반영했기 때문에 현재가 대비 0.7~1.2배 정도가 현실적이다.

주가로는 대략 70~120달러 구간이다.

Base Case에서는 좋은 회사지만 비싼 주식이다.

Bull Case.

Lead hyperscale customer의 production order가 반복되고, 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 Sonoma 수요가 더 커지며, silicon photonics customer도 follow-on order를 내는 경우다.

이 경우 FY2027~2028 매출이 현재 5,000만 달러 수준에서 1.5억~2.5억 달러 이상으로 올라갈 수 있다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 2~4배를 볼 수 있다.

주가로는 대략 200~400달러 구간이다.

이 시나리오에서는 AEHR이 단순 test equipment company가 아니라 AI ASIC과 silicon photonics burn-in 병목주로 재평가된다.

Extreme Bull Case.

AI hyperscaler들이 custom ASIC을 계속 확대하고, 고전력 AI processor burn-in이 필수 공정으로 자리 잡으며, silicon photonics transceiver WLBI와 SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in까지 동시에 ramp되는 경우다.

이 경우 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 시장에 모두 걸친 reliability test infrastructure company로 재평가될 수 있다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 5~8배까지도 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 500~800달러 구간이다.

다만 이건 매우 공격적인 상단 시나리오다.

이 시나리오가 나오려면 조건이 필요하다.

Lead hyperscale customer 주문이 반복되어야 한다.

AI ASIC 고객이 2개 이상으로 늘어나야 한다.

Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 이어져야 한다.

SiC/GaN burn-in 수요도 실제 매출로 붙어야 한다.

Gross margin과 operating margin이 장비주답게 올라와야 한다.

Consumables 매출이 반복적으로 쌓여야 한다.

AEHR을 SIVE/PENG/WOLF와 비교하면 꽤 흥미롭다.

SIVE는 photonics 제품 자체에 대한 고베타다.

상단은 크지만 아직 양산 매출과 ASP/물량 증거가 약하다.

PENG는 실적이 있는 AI infrastructure / Integrated Memory 회사다.

안정성은 좋지만 상단은 상대적으로 낮다.

WOLF는 SiC 전력반도체 턴어라운드다.

상단은 크지만 gross margin이 음수다.

AEHR은 그 중간 어딘가다.

SIVE보다 고객 주문이 더 직접적이다.

PENG보다 고베타다.

WOLF보다 현재 사업 구조가 덜 망가져 있다.

다만 AEHR도 이미 밸류에이션이 높고, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다.

그래서 내 기준 AEHR은 포트의 코어보다는 10~15% 고베타 병목 추가 후보에 가깝다.

AEHR의 핵심 체크포인트는 다음과 같다.

4,100만 달러 AI ASIC production order가 FY2027 매출로 계획대로 인식되는가.

Lead hyperscale customer의 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 추가 주문이 나오는가.

Silicon photonics 고객이 qualification에서 high-volume production으로 넘어가는가.

Follow-on orders가 실제로 나오는가.

Sonoma 시스템 생산능력 확대가 매출 전환을 뒷받침하는가.

Consumables, sockets, BIMs 매출이 반복적으로 쌓이는가.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 고객 주문으로 이어지는가.

Gross margin과 operating margin이 매출 증가와 함께 올라오는가.

고객이 한두 곳에 과도하게 집중되지 않는가.

현재 높은 valuation을 정당화할 만큼 FY2027 가이던스가 크게 올라가는가.

Kill Conditions도 분명하다.

Lead hyperscale customer 추가 주문이 지연되면 Bull Case는 약해진다.

4,100만 달러 주문의 납품 또는 매출 인식이 밀리면 주가는 크게 흔들릴 수 있다.

Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 넘어가지 못하면 광학 병목 논지는 약해진다.

FY2027 매출 가이던스가 시장 기대보다 낮으면 valuation 부담이 커진다.

Gross margin이 기대보다 낮게 나오면 장비주 프리미엄은 낮아진다.

고객 집중도가 높고 신규 고객 확장이 느리면 멀티플 확장은 제한된다.

SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 주문으로 연결되지 않으면 시장 확장 논지는 축소된다.

현재 시총이 이미 높기 때문에 작은 실망에도 조정이 클 수 있다.

최종 결론은 이렇다.

AEHR은 매우 흥미로운 병목주다.

이 회사는 AI ASIC, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power semiconductor라는 세 개의 고성장 병목 레이어에 걸린다.

특히 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order와 silicon photonics customer win은 단순 테마가 아니라 실제 고객 주문이라는 점에서 강하다.

이 점은 SIVE보다 더 직접적인 근거다.

다만 현재 매출은 아직 작고, 시총은 이미 30억 달러를 넘었다.

따라서 AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다.

정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics burn-in 매출이 폭발적으로 늘어날 경우 재평가될 수 있는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다.

AEHR의 첫 번째 핵심 축은 custom AI processor ASIC package-level burn-in이다. 이건 SIVE나 AXTI와 다르다. SIVE는 AI optical link용 DFB laser와 photonics 협력 근거가 있다. 하지만 아직 대량 양산 매출이 충분히 확인되지는 않았다. AXTI는 InP substrate 병목 논지가 강하다. 하지만 고객별 물량과 최종 플랫폼 노출은 더 확인해야 한다. 반면 AEHR은 적어도 AI processor ASIC burn-in 쪽에서는 대형 고객의 production order가 이미 찍힌 상태다. 이 점 때문에 AEHR은 새 후보 중에서 꽤 눈에 띈다. AEHR의 두 번째 축은 silicon photonics wafer-level burn-in이다. 2026년 3월 AEHR은 silicon photonics 분야의 major new customer로부터 주문을 받았다고 발표했다. 해당 고객은 글로벌 networking products and solutions 리더이자, data center optical transceiver 시장의 주요 공급자라고 설명됐다. 이 고객은 hyperscale AI와 cloud data center의 high-speed fiber optic communication link 수요를 겨냥해 silicon photonics 기반 transceiver를 개발 중이다. 이 주문에는 engineering qualification과 high-volume production을 위한 시스템들이 포함됐다. 이건 CPO/광학 병목과 직접 연결된다. AI 데이터센터가 copper에서 fiber optic interconnect로 이동하면 silicon photonics transceiver의 신뢰성 검증이 중요해진다. AEHR은 이 silicon photonics 제조 흐름에서 wafer-level burn-in이 점점 중요해질 수 있다고 보고 있다. 즉 AEHR은 AI ASIC burn-in + silicon photonics burn-in을 동시에 갖고 있다. 이 조합이 좋다. 지금 AI 병목 흐름은 CPO/광학, AI 데이터센터 전력, AI ASIC, silicon photonics 쪽으로 퍼지고 있다. AEHR은 이 중 두 개 이상에 직접 걸린다. 광학이 커지면 silicon photonics WLBI 수요가 생긴다. AI ASIC이 커지면 high-power package-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다. SiC/GaN power semiconductor가 커지면 wafer-level burn-in 수요가 생긴다. 즉 AEHR은 단일 테마가 아니라 여러 병목 레이어의 test infrastructure에 걸린 회사다. 하지만 현재 실적만 보면 아직 작다. FY2026 Q3 매출은 약 1,030만 달러였다. 전년 동기보다 낮다. GAAP 순손실도 있었고, Non-GAAP 기준으로도 손실이 남아 있다. 즉 AEHR은 아직 “실적이 이미 폭발한 회사”가 아니다. 오히려 현재 매출은 작고, 주가는 미래 주문과 FY2027 매출 전환을 선반영하고 있다. 이게 핵심이다. AEHR은 현재 손익보다 bookings와 FY2027 매출 전환을 봐야 하는 종목이다. 최근 bookings는 강하다. FY2026 Q3 bookings는 약 3,720만 달러였고, 이후 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order가 추가되면서 fiscal second half bookings가 9,200만 달러를 넘었다. 이 구조가 AEHR의 핵심이다. 현재 매출은 작지만, 주문이 앞에서 쌓이고 있다. 그래서 이 회사는 “현재 실적주”가 아니라 FY2027 이후 주문이 매출로 바뀌는지를 보는 고베타 병목주다. 현재 밸류에이션은 높다. 최근 기준 AEHR 주가는 약 99달러대, 시가총액은 약 30억 달러 수준이다. 회사의 FY2026 매출은 대략 4,500만~5,000만 달러 범위로 예상된다. 즉 현재 매출 대비 시총은 매우 높다. 이건 싸다고 볼 수 없다. AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다. 정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics / SiC-GaN burn-in 수요가 매출로 크게 전환된다는 기대를 사는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다. 그래도 볼 만한 이유는 명확하다. 첫째, 고객 주문의 질이 좋다. 4,100만 달러 주문은 단순 평가용 주문이 아니라 high-volume production burn-in용 주문이다. 대상도 custom AI processor ASIC이고, 고객은 lead hyperscale customer다. 이건 병목주에서 매우 강한 근거다. 둘째, consumables 모델이 있다. 주문에는 burn-in modules와 device-specific sockets가 포함된다. 이런 부품은 시스템 설치 이후에도 고객 ramp, 차세대 ASIC 전환, 제품별 세팅에 따라 반복 매출이 생길 수 있다. 장비를 팔고 끝나는 구조가 아닐 가능성이 있다. 셋째, silicon photonics와도 연결된다. AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect가 커질수록 silicon photonics transceiver 품질 검증이 중요해진다. AEHR은 이미 major networking / optical transceiver customer를 확보했고, qualification과 high-volume production용 시스템을 주문받았다. 넷째, SiC/GaN power semiconductor test까지 옵션이 있다. AI processor, silicon photonics, silicon carbide, gallium nitride power semiconductor wafer-level production burn-in이 모두 잠재 시장이다. 즉 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 방향에 걸친다. 다섯째, 생산능력 확장도 진행 중이다. 회사는 Fremont 시설에 power, cooling, clean-room manufacturing space를 추가했고, Sonoma 시스템 생산을 위한 contract manufacturing capacity도 준비 중이다. 주문이 들어왔을 때 생산이 가능해야 하는데, 이 부분을 준비하고 있다는 점은 긍정적이다. 하지만 리스크도 크다. 첫째, 현재 매출 대비 시총이 매우 높다. FY2026 매출 5,000만 달러 안팎 회사가 시총 30억 달러 이상에서 거래된다. 즉 시장은 FY2027~2028 매출 램프를 강하게 선반영하고 있다. 둘째, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다. Lead hyperscale customer 하나의 주문이 매우 중요하다. 이 고객의 ramp가 늦어지거나, 차세대 ASIC 일정이 지연되거나, 테스트 방식이 바뀌면 AEHR 매출이 크게 흔들릴 수 있다. 셋째, 주문은 매출이 아니다. Bookings가 좋다고 바로 매출과 이익이 되는 것은 아니다. 시스템 납품, 고객 acceptance, revenue recognition, production ramp가 필요하다. 넷째, silicon photonics도 아직 초기 ramp다. Major customer win은 좋지만, 전체 silicon photonics WLBI 시장이 얼마나 커질지는 확인이 필요하다. 다섯째, 아직 적자 회사다. 매출이 늘면 operating leverage가 생길 수 있지만, 지금은 아직 손실이 남아 있다. 여섯째, 주가가 이미 많이 올랐다. 현재 가격은 좋은 뉴스 상당 부분을 반영한다. 추가 주문이 계속 나오지 않으면 주가가 쉬어갈 수 있다. 시나리오는 이렇게 본다. Base Case. AEHR이 FY2027에 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC 주문과 silicon photonics 주문을 매출로 전환하지만, 추가 고객 확장이 생각보다 느린 경우다. 이 경우 현재 시총은 이미 기대를 많이 반영했기 때문에 현재가 대비 0.7~1.2배 정도가 현실적이다. 주가로는 대략 70~120달러 구간이다. Base Case에서는 좋은 회사지만 비싼 주식이다. Bull Case. Lead hyperscale customer의 production order가 반복되고, 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 Sonoma 수요가 더 커지며, silicon photonics customer도 follow-on order를 내는 경우다. 이 경우 FY2027~2028 매출이 현재 5,000만 달러 수준에서 1.5억~2.5억 달러 이상으로 올라갈 수 있다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 2~4배를 볼 수 있다. 주가로는 대략 200~400달러 구간이다. 이 시나리오에서는 AEHR이 단순 test equipment company가 아니라 AI ASIC과 silicon photonics burn-in 병목주로 재평가된다. Extreme Bull Case. AI hyperscaler들이 custom ASIC을 계속 확대하고, 고전력 AI processor burn-in이 필수 공정으로 자리 잡으며, silicon photonics transceiver WLBI와 SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in까지 동시에 ramp되는 경우다. 이 경우 AEHR은 AI compute, AI optical, AI power semiconductor 세 시장에 모두 걸친 reliability test infrastructure company로 재평가될 수 있다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 5~8배까지도 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 500~800달러 구간이다. 다만 이건 매우 공격적인 상단 시나리오다. 이 시나리오가 나오려면 조건이 필요하다. Lead hyperscale customer 주문이 반복되어야 한다. AI ASIC 고객이 2개 이상으로 늘어나야 한다. Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 이어져야 한다. SiC/GaN burn-in 수요도 실제 매출로 붙어야 한다. Gross margin과 operating margin이 장비주답게 올라와야 한다. Consumables 매출이 반복적으로 쌓여야 한다. AEHR을 SIVE/PENG/WOLF와 비교하면 꽤 흥미롭다. SIVE는 photonics 제품 자체에 대한 고베타다. 상단은 크지만 아직 양산 매출과 ASP/물량 증거가 약하다. PENG는 실적이 있는 AI infrastructure / Integrated Memory 회사다. 안정성은 좋지만 상단은 상대적으로 낮다. WOLF는 SiC 전력반도체 턴어라운드다. 상단은 크지만 gross margin이 음수다. AEHR은 그 중간 어딘가다. SIVE보다 고객 주문이 더 직접적이다. PENG보다 고베타다. WOLF보다 현재 사업 구조가 덜 망가져 있다. 다만 AEHR도 이미 밸류에이션이 높고, 고객 집중 리스크가 있다. 그래서 내 기준 AEHR은 포트의 코어보다는 10~15% 고베타 병목 추가 후보에 가깝다. AEHR의 핵심 체크포인트는 다음과 같다. 4,100만 달러 AI ASIC production order가 FY2027 매출로 계획대로 인식되는가. Lead hyperscale customer의 차세대 AI accelerator ASIC용 추가 주문이 나오는가. Silicon photonics 고객이 qualification에서 high-volume production으로 넘어가는가. Follow-on orders가 실제로 나오는가. Sonoma 시스템 생산능력 확대가 매출 전환을 뒷받침하는가. Consumables, sockets, BIMs 매출이 반복적으로 쌓이는가. SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 고객 주문으로 이어지는가. Gross margin과 operating margin이 매출 증가와 함께 올라오는가. 고객이 한두 곳에 과도하게 집중되지 않는가. 현재 높은 valuation을 정당화할 만큼 FY2027 가이던스가 크게 올라가는가. Kill Conditions도 분명하다. Lead hyperscale customer 추가 주문이 지연되면 Bull Case는 약해진다. 4,100만 달러 주문의 납품 또는 매출 인식이 밀리면 주가는 크게 흔들릴 수 있다. Silicon photonics 고객이 high-volume production으로 넘어가지 못하면 광학 병목 논지는 약해진다. FY2027 매출 가이던스가 시장 기대보다 낮으면 valuation 부담이 커진다. Gross margin이 기대보다 낮게 나오면 장비주 프리미엄은 낮아진다. 고객 집중도가 높고 신규 고객 확장이 느리면 멀티플 확장은 제한된다. SiC/GaN power semiconductor burn-in 수요가 실제 주문으로 연결되지 않으면 시장 확장 논지는 축소된다. 현재 시총이 이미 높기 때문에 작은 실망에도 조정이 클 수 있다. 최종 결론은 이렇다. AEHR은 매우 흥미로운 병목주다. 이 회사는 AI ASIC, silicon photonics, SiC/GaN power semiconductor라는 세 개의 고성장 병목 레이어에 걸린다. 특히 4,100만 달러 hyperscale AI customer production order와 silicon photonics customer win은 단순 테마가 아니라 실제 고객 주문이라는 점에서 강하다. 이 점은 SIVE보다 더 직접적인 근거다. 다만 현재 매출은 아직 작고, 시총은 이미 30억 달러를 넘었다. 따라서 AEHR은 저평가주가 아니다. 정확히는 FY2027 이후 AI ASIC / silicon photonics burn-in 매출이 폭발적으로 늘어날 경우 재평가될 수 있는 고밸류 고베타 병목주다."}}}, "rest_id": "2055734918928572566", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1863", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$TSEM / Tower Semiconductor 상세분석

Tower Semiconductor는 silicon photonics foundry 병목에서 가장 강한 증거가 찍힌 종목 중 하나다.

이 회사는 AI GPU를 만드는 회사가 아니다.

Optical module 완제품을 만드는 회사도 아니다.

하지만 AI 데이터센터 optical networking에서 silicon photonics 생산능력이 병목이 되면, Tower 같은 specialty foundry가 핵심이 된다.

Tower의 핵심은 SiPho foundry capacity다.

AI 데이터센터가 커질수록 optical interconnect와 silicon photonics 수요가 급증한다.

특히 InP laser shortage와 EML 공급 부족이 심해질수록, SiPho 기반 구조의 중요성은 더 커진다.

이 과정에서 silicon photonics wafer capacity를 제공하는 foundry가 병목이 될 수 있다.

Tower가 바로 여기에 있다.

최근 Tower는 매우 강한 신호를 보여줬다.

2027년 silicon photonics 매출 13억 달러 규모의 고객 계약을 체결했고, 고객으로부터 capacity reservation 선지급금 2.9억 달러를 받았다.

이건 단순 스토리가 아니다.

고객이 실제로 미래 생산능력을 선점하기 위해 돈을 냈다.

병목주에서 가장 강한 증거 중 하나는 고객이 capacity를 예약하려고 선지급금을 낸다는 점이다.

Tower는 그 증거가 있다.

현재 TSEM 주가는 약 274달러대, 시가총액은 약 304억 달러 수준이다.

이미 많이 올랐다.

하지만 Tower의 경우 단순 valuation보다 2027~2028년 SiPho 계약의 매출화가 핵심이다.

Base Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B SiPho 계약이 시장에 상당 부분 반영되고, 주가는 현재가 근처에서 실적 확인을 기다리는 경우다.

현재가 대비 약 0.9~1.2배 정도를 본다.

주가로는 대략 247~329달러 구간이다.

Bull Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B 계약이 순조롭게 매출화되고, 2028년 더 큰 wafer commitment와 추가 capacity reservation이 이어지는 경우다.

이 경우 Tower는 SiPho foundry 병목주로 완전히 재평가될 수 있다.

현재가 대비 약 1.6~2.5배까지 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 438~685달러 구간이다.

Extreme Bull Case에서는 SiPho가 AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect의 핵심 생산 플랫폼으로 자리 잡고, Tower가 주요 고객들의 capacity reservation을 계속 확보하며, 2028~2030년까지 장기 매출 visibility를 갖는 경우다.

이 경우 현재가 대비 약 3~4배까지 가능하다.

주가로는 대략 820~1,095달러 구간이다.

다만 이 시나리오에는 실행 리스크가 있다.

$1.3B 계약이 매출로 부드럽게 전환되어야 한다.

2028년 추가 계약이 더 커져야 한다.

고객사 선지급이 실제 수요를 반영해야 한다.

SiPho capacity가 계획대로 확장되어야 한다.

Margin이 유지되어야 한다.

리스크는 명확하다.

이미 주가가 많이 올랐다.

대형 계약은 실행 리스크가 있다.

고객 집중도가 높을 가능성이 있다.

SiPho 수요가 예상보다 약하거나 고객사 ramp가 지연되면 주가가 흔들릴 수 있다.

Foundry capacity expansion에는 capex와 yield 리스크가 있다.

결론적으로 TSEM은 이번 리스트에서 고객 증거가 가장 강한 종목 중 하나다.

LITE와 COHR이 laser·optical component 쪽이라면, Tower는 silicon photonics foundry capacity 쪽이다.

고객이 선지급금을 냈다는 점은 매우 강한 신호다.

다만 이미 많이 올랐다.

그래서 TSEM은 저평가주가 아니라, SiPho capacity 계약이 실제 매출로 전환되는지를 확인해야 하는 AI optical foundry 병목주다.

개인적인 분석 기록이며, 매수·매도 권유가 아니다.

Optical module 완제품을 만드는 회사도 아니다. 하지만 AI 데이터센터 optical networking에서 silicon", "id_str": "2055725817209233471", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "ko", "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "1614496714030993414"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [{"indices": [0, 5], "text": "TSEM"}], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNTU3MjU4MTcwNDk4OTQ5MTI=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "$TSEM / Tower Semiconductor 상세분석 Tower Semiconductor는 silicon photonics foundry 병목에서 가장 강한 증거가 찍힌 종목 중 하나다. 이 회사는 AI GPU를 만드는 회사가 아니다. Optical module 완제품을 만드는 회사도 아니다. 하지만 AI 데이터센터 optical networking에서 silicon photonics 생산능력이 병목이 되면, Tower 같은 specialty foundry가 핵심이 된다. Tower의 핵심은 SiPho foundry capacity다. AI 데이터센터가 커질수록 optical interconnect와 silicon photonics 수요가 급증한다. 특히 InP laser shortage와 EML 공급 부족이 심해질수록, SiPho 기반 구조의 중요성은 더 커진다. 이 과정에서 silicon photonics wafer capacity를 제공하는 foundry가 병목이 될 수 있다. Tower가 바로 여기에 있다. 최근 Tower는 매우 강한 신호를 보여줬다. 2027년 silicon photonics 매출 13억 달러 규모의 고객 계약을 체결했고, 고객으로부터 capacity reservation 선지급금 2.9억 달러를 받았다. 이건 단순 스토리가 아니다. 고객이 실제로 미래 생산능력을 선점하기 위해 돈을 냈다. 병목주에서 가장 강한 증거 중 하나는 고객이 capacity를 예약하려고 선지급금을 낸다는 점이다. Tower는 그 증거가 있다. 현재 TSEM 주가는 약 274달러대, 시가총액은 약 304억 달러 수준이다. 이미 많이 올랐다. 하지만 Tower의 경우 단순 valuation보다 2027~2028년 SiPho 계약의 매출화가 핵심이다. Base Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B SiPho 계약이 시장에 상당 부분 반영되고, 주가는 현재가 근처에서 실적 확인을 기다리는 경우다. 현재가 대비 약 0.9~1.2배 정도를 본다. 주가로는 대략 247~329달러 구간이다. Bull Case에서는 2027년 $1.3B 계약이 순조롭게 매출화되고, 2028년 더 큰 wafer commitment와 추가 capacity reservation이 이어지는 경우다. 이 경우 Tower는 SiPho foundry 병목주로 완전히 재평가될 수 있다. 현재가 대비 약 1.6~2.5배까지 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 438~685달러 구간이다. Extreme Bull Case에서는 SiPho가 AI 데이터센터 optical interconnect의 핵심 생산 플랫폼으로 자리 잡고, Tower가 주요 고객들의 capacity reservation을 계속 확보하며, 2028~2030년까지 장기 매출 visibility를 갖는 경우다. 이 경우 현재가 대비 약 3~4배까지 가능하다. 주가로는 대략 820~1,095달러 구간이다. 다만 이 시나리오에는 실행 리스크가 있다. $1.3B 계약이 매출로 부드럽게 전환되어야 한다. 2028년 추가 계약이 더 커져야 한다. 고객사 선지급이 실제 수요를 반영해야 한다. SiPho capacity가 계획대로 확장되어야 한다. Margin이 유지되어야 한다. 리스크는 명확하다. 이미 주가가 많이 올랐다. 대형 계약은 실행 리스크가 있다. 고객 집중도가 높을 가능성이 있다. SiPho 수요가 예상보다 약하거나 고객사 ramp가 지연되면 주가가 흔들릴 수 있다. Foundry capacity expansion에는 capex와 yield 리스크가 있다. 결론적으로 TSEM은 이번 리스트에서 고객 증거가 가장 강한 종목 중 하나다. LITE와 COHR이 laser·optical component 쪽이라면, Tower는 silicon photonics foundry capacity 쪽이다. 고객이 선지급금을 냈다는 점은 매우 강한 신호다. 다만 이미 많이 올랐다. 그래서 TSEM은 저평가주가 아니라, SiPho capacity 계약이 실제 매출로 전환되는지를 확인해야 하는 AI optical foundry 병목주다. 개인적인 분석 기록이며, 매수·매도 권유가 아니다."}}}, "rest_id": "2055725817209233471", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "148", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Marvell's primary CPO design is centered on its 6.4T 3D Silicon Photonics (SiPho) Light Engine — the industry’s first demonstrated 200 Gbps-per-lane optical engine for co-packaged optics (announced and live-demonstrated at OFC 2024). This is a fabless-designed, highly integrated optical “chiplet” that Marvell licenses or supplies to hyperscalers and custom XPU customers. $MRVL 

It serves as the foundational building block for both:
- CPO integration directly into custom AI accelerators (XPUs + HBM + optics on one substrate), and
- Linear-drive pluggable optics (LPO) or on-board optics as a bridge solution.

Core Technical Specs of the 6.4T 3D SiPho Light Engine

- Bandwidth: 6.4 Tbps aggregate (32 lanes × 200 Gbps PAM4, both electrical and optical interfaces).

- Power efficiency: < 5 pJ/bit (including external laser power) under typical conditions — roughly 30% lower per bit than previous 100G-generation engines.

- Integration level: Hundreds of components consolidated into a single compact package:
    -- Photonic IC (PIC): Silicon photonics waveguides, modulators, germanium photodetectors (Ge PDs), and fiber couplers.
    -- Electronic IC (EIC): Linear modulator drivers, transimpedance amplifiers (TIAs), equalization, and high-speed SerDes interfaces.
    -- Embedded microcontroller + firmware: For real-time calibration, monitoring, thermal stabilization, and link management.
    -- Passives, power distribution, and test circuitry.

- Laser: External continuous-wave (CW) laser source (fiber-delivered) for thermal and reliability reasons.

-Fiber attach: Supports detachable optical connectors (e.g., SENKO’s 36-channel metallic PIC connector) for field serviceability — critical when a 100T+ system may require thousands of fibers.

- Packaging: Advanced 3D heterogeneous integration (EIC face-to-face or side-by-side with PIC, often using TSVs, hybrid bonding, or interposers). The engine has a BGA-style electrical interface for direct attachment to the host substrate.

How It Integrates with Custom XPU Architectures

Marvell’s breakthrough CPO architecture (announced early 2025) lets customers build a single substrate that co-packages:
- One or more XPU compute dies
- HBM memory stacks
- Multiple 6.4T (or 1.6T) SiPho light engines
- High-speed SerDes + die-to-die interfaces

The light engines sit right next to the compute dies. Optical fibers exit the package via detachable connectors, enabling rack-scale or row-scale optical connectivity without copper cables. This delivers:
- 2× bandwidth density vs. 100G solutions
- Dramatically lower power and latency
- Easier thermal management (optics can be placed optimally)

Post-Acquisition Photonic Fabric™

Marvell acquired Celestial AI to accelerate scale-up interconnects. Their PFLink™ (Photonic Fabric Link) chiplet builds on similar CPO principles but targets even higher performance:
- First-gen: 16 Tbps per chiplet
- Uses EAM (electro-absorption) modulators on the PIC + TSMC 4/5nm EIC
- In-package optics + memory semantic FLITs + in-network processing
- Enables multi-rack optical fabrics with <150 ns XPU-to-XPU latency and ~2.5 pJ/bit full link power

This is a fabless-designed, highly integrated optical “chiplet” that Marvell licenses or supplies to hyperscalers and custom XPU customers. $MRVL It serves as the foundational building block for both: - CPO integration directly into custom AI accelerators (XPUs + HBM + optics on one substrate), and - Linear-drive pluggable optics (LPO) or on-board optics as a bridge solution. Core Technical Specs of the 6.4T 3D SiPho Light Engine - Bandwidth: 6.4 Tbps aggregate (32 lanes × 200 Gbps PAM4, both electrical and optical interfaces). - Power efficiency: < 5 pJ/bit (including external laser power) under typical conditions — roughly 30% lower per bit than previous 100G-generation engines. - Integration level: Hundreds of components consolidated into a single compact package: -- Photonic IC (PIC): Silicon photonics waveguides, modulators, germanium photodetectors (Ge PDs), and fiber couplers. -- Electronic IC (EIC): Linear modulator drivers, transimpedance amplifiers (TIAs), equalization, and high-speed SerDes interfaces. -- Embedded microcontroller + firmware: For real-time calibration, monitoring, thermal stabilization, and link management. -- Passives, power distribution, and test circuitry. - Laser: External continuous-wave (CW) laser source (fiber-delivered) for thermal and reliability reasons. -Fiber attach: Supports detachable optical connectors (e.g., SENKO’s 36-channel metallic PIC connector) for field serviceability — critical when a 100T+ system may require thousands of fibers. - Packaging: Advanced 3D heterogeneous integration (EIC face-to-face or side-by-side with PIC, often using TSVs, hybrid bonding, or interposers). The engine has a BGA-style electrical interface for direct attachment to the host substrate. How It Integrates with Custom XPU Architectures Marvell’s breakthrough CPO architecture (announced early 2025) lets customers build a single substrate that co-packages: - One or more XPU compute dies - HBM memory stacks - Multiple 6.4T (or 1.6T) SiPho light engines - High-speed SerDes + die-to-die interfaces The light engines sit right next to the compute dies. Optical fibers exit the package via detachable connectors, enabling rack-scale or row-scale optical connectivity without copper cables. This delivers: - 2× bandwidth density vs. 100G solutions - Dramatically lower power and latency - Easier thermal management (optics can be placed optimally) Post-Acquisition Photonic Fabric™ Marvell acquired Celestial AI to accelerate scale-up interconnects. Their PFLink™ (Photonic Fabric Link) chiplet builds on similar CPO principles but targets even higher performance: - First-gen: 16 Tbps per chiplet - Uses EAM (electro-absorption) modulators on the PIC + TSMC 4/5nm EIC - In-package optics + memory semantic FLITs + in-network processing - Enables multi-rack optical fabrics with <150 ns XPU-to-XPU latency and ~2.5 pJ/bit full link power"}}}, Short on-package electrical https://t.co/kbjVtF7Qkh", "id_str": "2055006412573728946", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 1, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "174073808"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNTUwMDY0MTI0MTg1MTkwNDA=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "How the CPO Process Works (Step-by-Step Signal Flow) Here's the typical end-to-end flow in a CPO system: 1. Electrical signals leave the host chip (e.g., switch ASIC or XPU): High-speed SerDes (serializer/deserializer) outputs electrical data. 2. Short on-package electrical interconnect to the optical engine (usually via micro-bumps, copper pillars, or redistribution layers on the substrate/interposer). This path is only mm long → minimal power loss and signal degradation. 3. Electronic IC (EIC) in the optical engine: Receives the electrical signal and drives the photonic components (e.g., modulator drivers, transimpedance amplifiers/TIAs). 4. Photonic IC (PIC) does the conversion: - Transmit (Tx): Continuous-wave laser light (usually external, via fiber) enters the PIC. The EIC drives modulators (e.g., micro-ring modulators/MRMs) on the PIC to encode the data onto the light. - Receive (Rx): Incoming light from fiber hits photodetectors on the PIC, which convert it to electrical current. The EIC amplifies and processes it. 5. Fiber coupling: Light enters/exits the PIC via precise couplers (grating couplers with micro-lenses or edge couplers) connected to a fiber array unit (FAU). Fibers then run to the chassis faceplate or other packages. 6. Back to the host chip on the receive side (short electrical path again). The optical engine is typically a 3D-stacked or side-by-side EIC + PIC die (often using hybrid bonding or 2.5D/3D-IC tech). The whole thing sits on an organic substrate or silicon interposer alongside the main ASIC, with external lasers (for reliability/thermal reasons) or sometimes integrated ones. Key enablers: - Silicon photonics (SiPh) on the PIC: Waveguides, MRMs (tiny resonant rings for efficient, compact 200 Gbps+ modulation), germanium photodetectors. - Advanced packaging: 2.5D (interposer), 3D stacking, CoWoS (Chip on Wafer on Substrate), etc. - Fiber attach: Sub-micron precision alignment (challenging but solved with micro-lenses/grating couplers)."}}}, "rest_id": "2055006412573728946", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "239", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2055012160820891651", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "133", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: @aleabitoreddit What do you think of $FABC they're also in photonics

Will this have huge impact on on $sive? Cpo stock down a lot today due to this news? “TSMC's COUPE (Compact Universal Photonic Engine) is a cutting-edge silicon photonics platform designed for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), aimed at accelerating chip-to-chip communication in AI data centers. By directly integrating optical engines with high-performance compute silicon (like GPUs or switches) on a 2.5D/3D package, COUPE dramatically reduces power consumption and boosts data bandwidth, addressing the limitations of traditional copper-based interconnects. 

Ansys
 +4
Key Function: COUPE uses SOIC-X to stack an electrical die directly on top of a photonic die, resulting in a compact "engine" that enables ultra-high-density interconnects.
Production Roadmap: TSMC plans to introduce COUPE-based products in 2025 for validation, with mass production scheduled for 2026 integrated into its CoWoS-integrated CPO packages.
Performance Impact: This technology aims to deliver a 2x reduction in power consumption and a 10x reduction in latency compared to existing solutions.
Market Impact: The technology is heavily supported by major industry players like NVIDIA and AMD for their next-generation AI platforms.
Technical Advantages: COUPE features embedded micro-lens arrays, a backside metal reflector, and a grating coupler to minimize optical insertion loss, keeping it below 1.2 dB. 

Design And Reuse
 +6
By moving from pluggable transceivers to on-package CPO using COUPE, data centers can achieve significant power savings, especially in massive AI clusters where data movement is the primary energy cost. “

text:
The US Photonics Supply Chain: The Unsung Hero Powering AI’s Explosive Growth While everyone focuses on GPUs, the real bottleneck — and opportunity — lies in the optical interconnects that move massive amounts of data at light speed inside AI data centers. Gemini Trading’s infographic maps the complete US Photonics Supply Chain for AI infrastructure:  Key Layers:InP Substrates → AXT (global leader)
Active Optical Chips → Lumentum, Coherent, Broadcom, MACOM
TOSA/ROSA & Optical Engines → Core components for high-speed transmission
Optical Modules & AOC → AAOI, Ciena, Fabrinet
Networking Systems → Arista, Broadcom, NVIDIA
Terminal AI Applications → NVIDIA GB200 (paired with 18x 800G optical modules), Quantum-X CPO

Market Data:
Global Optical Module Market expected to grow from US$51 billion in 2024 to US$89 billion in 2029 at a 12.5% CAGR. DSP components alone make up 30-40% of an 800G module’s BOM. Why it matters:
AI training clusters are scaling to millions of GPUs. Traditional copper can’t keep up — photonics (light-based data transfer) is the only solution for low-latency, high-bandwidth, energy-efficient connectivity at scale. 2025–2026 will see large-scale deployment of 1.6T systems. Investment angle:
Companies across the photonics stack — especially those enabling 800G/1.6T modules and co-packaged optics (CPO) — stand to benefit enormously as AI capex continues surging. The photonic supply chain is still under-the-radar compared to GPU hype, but it may deliver some of the strongest multi-year growth in the entire AI ecosystem.(Not financial advice — always DYOR and manage risk appropriately.)@GeminiTrading

Gemini Trading’s infographic maps the complete US Photonics Supply Chain for AI infrastructure: Key Layers:InP Substrates → AXT (global leader) Active Optical Chips → Lumentum, Coherent, Broadcom, MACOM TOSA/ROSA & Optical Engines → Core components for high-speed transmission Optical Modules & AOC → AAOI, Ciena, Fabrinet Networking Systems → Arista, Broadcom, NVIDIA Terminal AI Applications → NVIDIA GB200 (paired with 18x 800G optical modules), Quantum-X CPO Market Data: Global Optical Module Market expected to grow from US$51 billion in 2024 to US$89 billion in 2029 at a 12.5% CAGR. Investment angle: Companies across the photonics stack — especially those enabling 800G/1.6T modules and co-packaged optics (CPO) — stand to benefit enormously as AI capex continues surging. text:
Nvidia isn’t just selling the picks and shovels — it’s quietly buying the entire gold mine.
By dropping billions into optics, power, photonics, and AI cloud players, Jensen is locking down the physical bottlenecks that could slow AI scaling. This isn’t random VC money; it’s vertical control that funnels more demand straight back to Nvidia GPUs while building a deeper moat.
Classic platform power move: own the dependencies. Expect the flywheel to spin harder.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

@ParadisLabs Just really interested in $PENG
1. Cause of the SKT connection
2. Is the larger contexts without buying more GPUs

Explained some here 
https://t.co/Rta4bZjx7p

The whole photonics memory thing is just retail doing retail
embedded_text:
From what I have read about $PENG we have the following:

→ CXL lets servers tap a shared pool of DRAM over a fast interconnect. 
→ KV cache is the running memory LLMs need during inference, it overflows GPU VRAM fast. 
Putting the KV cache in a CXL pool means longer contexts

"richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "When I see comments like this (and there are a lot) from retail investors: I immediately think they lack the technical depth. I'll walk through each one from $SIVE to $LPK: 1. Photonics TAM goes from $14B -> $154B In just two years time, and it's likely going to keep scaling past 2030 as it's the next generation architecture of choice. It's not going away in 1 year. It's not going away in 3 years, which is why $LITE premiums keep going higher since they're backlogged into 2028. $SIVE supplies CW lasers and is highly tethered to CPO and now pluggable transcivers for 1.6T and 3.2... For expected companies like $JBL, Ayar, Lightmatter, Lightelligence, $POET, $MRVL Celestial, and $AMD. This isn't a "trade", it's the core chokepoint and IP holder for the next generation of photonics. And it's a comfortable hold for the next few years as they scale to become the next $LITE. The risk I personally see (since they're already qualified with so many players), it's mainly how much TAM they can capture of the overall optical supercycle. (And potential risks with Win Semi volume ramp, but Win is massive so I can sleep tightly there). As just supplying lasers isn't enough to justify valuation. It's TAM expansion downward into making the entire ELS or entire pluggable transceiver that makes these laser companies so valuable. Then afterward, they can vertically integrating upward for gross margin expansion upward like $COHR into doing the laser fabs or even substrate level. And that in my view is a very asymmetric risk/reward ratio as we've already seen this done with $LITE as they went from $2B to $80B. 2. $LPK - Is the purest exposure, without the messy financials of SKC Absolics, as the next advanced packaging shift for glass substrates. Almost every single major semi company from $INTC to Samsung are adopting glass substrates. $LPK is basically $ASML of this chokepoint, since they supply to ~80% of the global players currently. Yes, there's "trade cycles" for equipment suppliers like $ASML, where if there's more foundry capex, ASML scales up. But if there's downturns, these tend to perform poorly, and don't capture all the volume ramp that happens after. However, if the MC is $650m and they're making $100-200M, revenue per costumer volume ramped, the amount they make from the glass substrate cycle will likely exceed current valuations. And they'll have baseline fundamentals (as more companies adopt the packaging shift), that keeps their valuation up. It's just a waiting game for volume ramp at this point. 3. $AAOI - This is literally $INTC but for America + Photonics. It's like saying Intel is not a long term investment. Guess where all your optical transcivers are made? China. Thailand. Malaysia. If you look at Innolight, Eoptolink, $FN, and others. AOI is building the largest Made in America supply chains for both CW laser fab, as well as 800g, 1.6T assembly. Yes, there are pluggable cycle ups and downs to this as well. There's going to be a wave for 1.6T next year, then CPO cannibalizes pluggables down the road. But since they make the entire supply chain in house, they have extreme optionality for other segments. And like $NVDA older gen-GPUs, there's going to be sovereign DC requirements for older gen pluggables from names like $AAOI. It's likely going to keep rising as it hits that $400m+/month revenue target H2 2026. There's just a lot of different short term volatility along the way like the $600m dilution. 4. $IQE - ??? It's one of the most important players in the Western word for epiwafers. $MTSI went out of their way to pay off IQE's debt because they can't have them going under. $IQE is also supplying to $LITE. The world is currently bottlenecked both on the epiwafer level from Landmark comments and InP substrate levels. Their financials were track but the raw book value, and value they hold to the entire Western supply chain... completely justifies their valuation. And other optical companies will not let their core upstream supply chain go under. As these tens of millions worth of materials would screw up tens of billions worth of downstream products. Again photonics is the next generation architecture required to scale AI. It's not Quantum where it's just "In development". It's literally here and the architecture of choice by $NVDA. I would not be surprised if all of these are a lot higher in 3-4 years time. People who think it's one and done in 3 months time "only because I mentioned it" don't know what they're talking about. Institutions would have bought up the name eventually (like Point 72 on $IQE) and retail would only find out after their valuations are 600% higher. Should really do the research before adding comments like these: These are all forward growth companies that require in-depth supply chain knowledge."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2055299648093340047"}}, "rest_id": "2055822766600016238", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "606237", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056079647406747852", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "5253", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

You decide for yourself if the bullish thesis holds. $AAOI $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $LITE

text:
When cars were invented, people didn’t stop riding horses overnight.

The better technology eventually won. But the transition took longer - and usually messier - than we expected.

#Photonics in #AI networking may be similar.

Optics IS the next major upgrade:
- lower power
- faster speeds
- better scaling for massive AI clusters

But copper still works well enough today:
- cheaper
- simpler
- still heavily used in many current AI clusters

That’s why #hyperscalers are still building large #copper-heavy systems today.

Photonics today looks more like an upgrade cycle than a hard #bottleneck like #EUV or advanced chip manufacturing.

The technology is improving quickly.

But the industry still needs time on:
- packaging
- reliability
- integration at scale
- deployment economics

We’ve seen similar setups before: #solar, #EV charging, fiber buildouts, early #5G.

The direction was right. 
The rollout was slower. 
Valuations reset hard.

 #photonics #optics #CPO #siliconphotonics will no doubt  become the “standard,” but..

IF the rollout takes longer too, 

how much of today’s valuation still holds up?

As investors we all want to believe we’ve found the next “certainty.”

Reality is rarely that clean.

Still worth thinking about.

"to_index": 2222}]}, "text": "There’s a lot to digest from the last week with $SIVE. The $JBL 1.6T collaboration, together with the confirmed CW laser shortage and partnerships with O-Net and Enablence Technologies, feels like a decisive step forward. In the Annual Report from May 13, $SIVE directly addresses “discussions with hyperscalers and pluggable transceiver suppliers” that point to a structural shortage of CW lasers. This is happening at exactly the right time, AI training and inference clusters are pushing explosive demand for higher bandwidth, lower power and denser interconnects. What really stands out to me is how the dots are connecting. $JBL, key partner to $AMZN, $MSFT and $GOOGL has chosen $SIVE’s high-performance InP DFB lasers for 1.6T pluggable modules. At the same time, Amazon’s recent private placement with AIChip (Ayar Labs’ lead customer) signals major design wins ahead. And $SIVE is the sole supplier of the critical laser arrays to Ayar Labs. The same pattern keeps repeating. O-Net Technologies (with strong North American and Asian hyperscaler ties) and Enablence are developing ELS modules with $SIVE lasers for CPO. POET Technologies $POET just secured a $50 million initial order from Lumilens. Everywhere the big players are moving, $SIVE seems to be part of the picture. On the manufacturing side, the WIN Semiconductors partnership together with the own Glasgow fab gives $SIVE real independence and scalable capacity (30k–35k wafers/year) in a very tight InP market. All of this is coming together right now. Pluggables give near-term volume, while CPO offers the big efficiency jump ahead. The photonics TAM is exploding from $14B to $154B in just two years and $SIVE is positioned right in the middle of it. With this level of validation across the value chain, I believe we are likely to see more partnerships and firm purchase orders soon. For $SIVE, this feels like a real inflection point. The company is transitioning from deep-tech innovator to a strategically critical, independent supplier in the optical backbone of next-generation AI. The hyperscalers keep choosing $SIVE for a reason. Execution is there, the connections are deepening, and this momentum feels increasingly unstoppable."}}}, "rest_id": "2056055516107866286", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "197447", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2056067956526760180", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2615", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
他说的非常对，总结下来就三点：

1. AI 光子学，他们的Photonics部门Q4 2025已实现调整后EBITDA转正至+1600万SEK。 该部已经实现自我造血功能，并不需要公司稀释股份来完成科研。

另外，Sivers与一家未披露的Fortune 100企业建立了长期合作，这是光子业务的核心锚点。 当然也包括我们的老客户 $POET，共同为AI数据中心交付创新光引擎。

2. 5G/6G FR3波束成形

 $sive 在3月份正式发布7-15GHz Daybreak™波束成形IC，面向5G/6G FR3新兴应用及多功能国防阵列。合作方包括雷神（Raytheon）和爱立信（Ericsson）。

3. 激光雷达

该项目的客户将于2026年Q4开始量产，使用Sivers的激光器和放大器。

三辆马车会把 $sive拉得更远……………

YOLO………

text:
the Evercore 1Q26 AI channel checks showing scale-out demand still strong and gated by supply, with scale-up interconnects converging fast toward optical but stuck on protocol and form-factor standardization, is the exact signal that the networking/photonics layer is becoming the next real choke point.

hyperscaler strategies diverging while the physical bottlenecks stay tight is bullish for $MRVL, $AVGO, $MTSI, $ALAB, and $NVDA, but the real edge stays with the names that can actually deliver into the supply-constrained reality.

this is structural, not hype.

hyperscalers are locked into hundreds of billions in committed cluster spend, and the shift to optical interconnects at scale isn’t optional anymore as clusters hit density, heat, and power limits copper can’t solve.

this cycle rewards the execution plays facing proven shortages and shipping product at scale. the AI buildout has years left and the interconnect layer is one of those constraints that can’t be fixed overnight. the setup stays strong.
