text:
上个帖子提到的未来将会成为下一个 $LITE的瑞典光子学股票

Sivers Photonics 是 CPO 领域重要的激光器供应商之一，通过供应链映射，它与 $LITE、 $COHR、 $MTSI、Sumitomo、Furukawa 等公司同处这一供应链版图中。

我非常看好 Sivers，因为我发现它映射到了许多超大规模云厂商的供应商链条中。而这些信息，其他人、算法以及更广泛的市场，目前还并不了解。

Sivers（ $SIVE）仍然是 Photonics/CPO 领域中一个并不广为人知、但非常关键的 CW 激光器瓶颈环节。

它映射到 Apple、AMD、Marvell、Amazon、Microsoft、Meta 等公司的客户链条。同时，CPO 是其主要增长向量，并通过 2027 年和 2028 年面向 1.6T/3.2T 的可插拔模块扩展其 TAM。

这份报告包含了一些信息发现，将 Sivers 映射到了许多领先但尚未披露的超大规模云厂商供应商。

公开披露的客户包括：

→ Jabil（NYSE: JBL）：“Sivers Semiconductors 与 Jabil 合作开发高能效 1.6T 可插拔光收发模块”

→ Poet（ $POET）：“POET Technologies 与 Sivers Semiconductors 合作，为共封装光学和下一代 AI 市场开发外部光源”

→ Ayar Labs： $SIVE 作为激光器供应商被列在 Ayar Labs 官网上。

→ O-Net 和 Enablence：“Sivers Semiconductors、O-Net 和 Enablence Technologies 宣布面向 AI 数据中心的外部光源”

→ LIGHTIUM AG（“将其 CW 激光器直接集成到 TFLN 晶圆上”）：2025 年年度报告 PDF。用于 1.6T / 3.2T 的激光器。

高置信度客户包括：

→ Apple（ $AAPL）——用于 Apple 硅光子项目的激光器，尤其是 Apple Watch。

→ Aeva（ $AEVA）——其他分析师报告中经常提到的 LiDAR 客户。

→ Marvell Celestial（ $MRVL）——直接供应商，与 $POET 无关。

可能客户包括：

→ Lightmatter（通过投资映射 + 图像演示资料）

→ Lightelligence（HKG: 1879）——通过投资映射 + 图像演示资料

→ Advanced Micro Devices（ $AMD）——通过 GlobalFoundries 的 CPO 项目公告“AMD Taps GlobalFoundries for MI500’s Co-Packaged Optics”以及激光器供应商映射。

在 CPO/光学供应链之外，Sivers 很可能也是以下客户的供应商：

→ YSS。Sivers 的主要客户 ALLSPACE 被 York 收购，现在已嵌入 YSS 供应链。

→ Golden Dome、Space Force、Space Development Agency 以及 DoD 相关关系，现在都通过 YSS 向上游映射到 Sivers。

其母公司的 CHIPS Act 拨款包括：

→ BAE Systems 等军工承包商，近期获得 660 万美元 CHIPS Act 第二年后续拨款。

→ 与 ERIC 和 Raytheon（RTX）合作的 FR3 波束成形器 IC。

此外，→ NOKIA 也可能是 Sivers 的另一个尚未被市场发现的客户，这一点来自其 2025 年年度报告中的收入/地点映射。

来自 Sivers 主要线索的一跳/两跳客户包括：

Ayar（CPO）：

→ AlChip / GUC / Winwynn，通过 Ayar。主要线索重点是 AlChip 的 CPO。

→ → Amazon 很可能是 Ayar 的主要生态客户：在 Amazon 于 2026 年 5 月进行新的私募投资之后，AlChip 可能赢得了 Trainium 4 设计。AlChip 是 Ayar 的主要客户。

→ → AMD 很有可能成为 Sivers 的主要客户。

可能通过两条路径实现：

Enosemi（AMD 收购后的内部 PIC 设计团队）。Enosemi 的 chiplets 在 GF 代工，但对于 ELS，AMD 可以从多个玩家采购，其中 $SIVE 可能是底层的多来源激光器来源。
Ayar（AMD 于 2026 年 3 月参与其 E 轮投资）。Ayar 的 SuperNova 光源已经使用 $SIVE 的 DFB 激光器阵列。考虑到时间线以及近期巨额融资，Ayar 的 SuperNova 可能是 2027 年 MI500 第一代 CPO 路径。它们都出现在 GFS 关于 AMD / GFS CPO 公告的生态系统幻灯片中，作为生态合作伙伴出现。

→ 其他超大规模云厂商，如 Meta、Microsoft、Google 等，也具有较高可能性。

在 Lumentum 和 Macom 从 Ayar 官网移除之后， $SIVE 很可能成为 Ayar 的主要激光器供应商。

Lumentum 也曾暗示，由于产能过度转向 Nvidia 所需的 EML，其 CW 激光器生产存在问题；根据其财报电话会议记录，他们正在从竞争对手的公开市场采购相关产品。

Sivers 也在其近期发布的年度报告中暗示了与 Win Semi 的产能分配确认，这很可能帮助其赢得 Ayar 的主要供应位置。

text:
the market pricing photonics transition like it’s imminent while the actual builders say copper-to-optics in a rack multiplies cost by x10 is the kind of disconnect that creates real opportunity for the names already shipping market-fit solutions today.

the post is spot on. Eric Cap from Bull/Eviden and the other panelists at the OCP Summit aren’t sell-side analysts; they’re the guys actually building the machines. 

when they say optics is too expensive right now, the pragmatic response is densification, liquid cooling, and specialized compute instead of waiting for photonics to get cheap.

that’s exactly why $AL2SI (2CRSi) and $ALKAL (KALRAY) look well positioned. ultra-dense thermal architecture and DPU MPPA solutions solve the constraints the industry is facing right now without waiting for the next-gen interconnect to drop in price.

i’m biased that this slower photonics ramp is net positive for the European plays that can deliver today. hyperscalers are locked into hundreds of billions in committed cluster spend and they need capacity now, not in 2-3 years when optics economics improve. 

the physical bottlenecks in power, heat, and interconnects don’t pause for the perfect technology; they get solved with what’s available and economical.

this cycle rewards the execution plays that solve proven shortages and ship product at scale right now, not the ones betting on a future cost curve. the setup for $AL2SI and $ALKAL has legs while the broader AI infra buildout keeps rolling.

The entire AI buildout sector is getting a pullback in advance of $NVDA earnings tomorrow. Obviously Nvidia is going to kill earnings & revenue. But NVDA is king and guidance will determine how much perceived demand there is for memory, photonics, data centers, etc.

So many AI buildout stocks are at the 21 EMA, with A+ retracement patterns. If guidance is perceived to be bullish we could see an overall continuation in the weeks to come.

text:
.@light_con, a developer of femtosecond lasers and laser systems, has acquired Class 5 Photonics, a Hamburg, Germany-based manufacturer of ultrafast laser systems.

Read more here: https://t.co/SlURte3EC9

#Photonics #Lasers

核心总结：Sivers Photonics（$SIVE）是 CPO（共封装光学）领域目前被市场严重低估的核心 CW 激光供应商，它已悄然深度映射到 Apple、AMD、Marvell Celestial、Amazon、Microsoft、Meta 等 hyperscaler 的供应链，并通过 Jabil、POET、Ayar Labs、O-Net、Lightmatter、Lightelligence 等关键伙伴形成广泛覆盖，涵盖可插拔 1.6T/3.2T 模块和共封装光学两大路线。作为激光这一 AI 数据中心光互连的瓶颈环节，SIVE 将直接受益于 2027-2028 年 CPO 超级周期的爆发式增长，具有极高的 TAM 扩张和业绩爆发潜力，目前多数高端客户关系仍未被市场充分认知。

text:
Real reason why $NVDA earnings matter so much tonight.

Not just the headline beat/miss.

It’s the forward commentary on:

• Rubin ramp
• Blackwell margins
• Quantum-X deployments
• Spectrum-X timing
• CPO / silicon photonics adoption
• HBM pricing & packaging constraints

Because Nvidia is the operating system of the entire AI infrastructure stack.

The biggest risk into earnings, in my opinion:

Margins.

If Rubin + GB300 ramps accelerate while HBM4 and advanced packaging costs stay elevated, the market may finally start questioning whether AI infra growth can continue without margin compression.

But if Nvidia guides cleanly above expectations again?

Then the AI capex supercycle is still alive — and semis probably squeeze even harder into H2.

It’s a macro event for the entire AI economy.

text:
$SIVE (Sivers Photonics) is my highest-conviction pick for the strongest stock appreciation in the entire photonics sector over the next 2–3 years.
Current price: ~45 SEK. Long-term target (2027–2028): 150–250 SEK (3–5x upside potential).

Sivers is a pure-play leader in high-performance CW DFB laser arrays; the critical light source enabling co-packaged optics (CPO) and high-speed pluggable modules for AI data centers.

Evidence and data backing the thesis:

AI interconnect demand explosion: As clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+, copper hits hard limits on heat, signal loss, and density. Optical interconnects are becoming mandatory, driving massive demand for lasers.

Strong ecosystem positioning: Partnerships and design wins with Ayar Labs (SuperNova), POET Technologies, Marvell Celestial, Jabil, and others. Recent CHIPS Act grant with BAE Systems and MIT Lincoln Lab adds U.S. government validation and non-dilutive funding.

Dual growth drivers: Primary: AI photonics. Secondary: defense, LiDAR, and satellite comms. This diversification reduces risk while the AI ramp accelerates.

Small-cap asymmetry: Low current revenue base means successful volume ramps can drive explosive growth. 

Analysts see significant revenue scaling as major CPO programs move into production in 2027–2028.

Qualitative edge: Sivers has spent years perfecting high-reliability lasers with the performance needed for AI-scale deployments. 

The combination of government backing, strong customer pipeline, and being European (reducing geopolitical risk) gives it a real moat in a market where supply chain security matters more every day.

Risks:
Execution on volume ramp and yield
Potential dilution if more capital is needed
Competition in a fast-moving space

$SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint for the next wave of AI interconnects. The photonics transition is structural and inevitable; Sivers is one of the best-positioned small-caps to benefit with real customer traction and government support.

This one ages very well. Worth accumulating on weakness.

Risks: Execution on volume ramp and yield Potential dilution if more capital is needed Competition in a fast-moving space $SIVE sits right in the middle of the laser chokepoint for the next wave of AI interconnects. text:
laser / photonics sector looks like one of the highest-conviction 6-month setups in the entire ai stack right now.

copper is hitting its physical limits fast as clusters scale to 800g and 1.6t+. heat, signal loss, and density issues are forcing hyperscalers to switch to optical interconnects at rack and campus scale. 

the data backs it up hard:
1.6t optical modules are moving from lab to commercial deployment in 2026

optical content per gpu is expected to rise 1.3–1.5x as scale-out and scale-across networks expand

silicon photonics market is forecasted to grow from ~$2.86b in 2025 to $28.75b by 2034 (29%+ cagr), with the biggest ramp hitting in the next 2–3 years

names like $LITE, $SIVE, $COHR, and $GLW sit right in the middle of the laser and optical engine supply chain with real design wins and qualification momentum. 

the ones that can deliver high-volume, high-reliability lasers and transceivers are going to see strong re-rating as hyperscalers pull harder.

the short-term risk is execution and any delay in volume ramps, but the structural demand is undeniable. 
this is not narrative; it’s the next physical bottleneck after memory and power.

the laser/photonics sector has one of the cleanest asymmetric setup for the next 6 months in my book.

text:
$LITE’s OCS tech being called an “overnight success” after 10 years of R&D (with heavy involvement from $SLXN and $P4O)  is exactly how the best photonics plays look right now.

the CEO’s answer on Chinese competitors was sharp; they’ve been iterating for a decade while others are still catching up. high-powered lasers originally developed for undersea applications are now being repurposed for co-packaged optics, which is the direction hyperscalers are heading as clusters scale to 800G/1.6T+.

i’m biased that this is the kind of deep technical moat that actually matters in the interconnect layer. 

hyperscalers are hitting real limits on heat, signal loss, and density that copper can’t solve. the names that have spent years perfecting lasers and optical circuit switches keep the structural edge.

the comments asking about other long-term OCS players like Huber-Suhner ($HUBN) acquiring Polatis in 2016 are right to look for similar multi-year development stories. 

the ones calling it “mandatory reading” get that this is not hype; it’s real manufacturing and qualification work that takes a decade.

this cycle rewards the execution plays solving proven physical bottlenecks that hyperscalers can’t pause for. 

photonics and OCS are one of those constraints right now, and $LITE (with the ecosystem around it) sits right in the middle. the setup has legs. this one ages well.

text:
最近光模块很热，尤其是DSP，CPO等相关的新一代“光芯片”技术，引领整个光产业的股票狂飙

YTD 表现里 AAOI +441%、LITE +166%、COHR +97%——同一个板块，分化 4 倍

而接下来 12-24 个月真正定生死的是 CPO 时间表：

2026 H2：NVDA 的 Quantum-X / Spectrum-X Photonics 首批 scale-out CPO switch 量产
2027 H2：Rubin Ultra 整机 scale-up CPO 首批出货

这两个节点 TAM 完全不在一个量级。按 Lumentum 自己披露的数据，Rubin Ultra 架构里 scale-up:scale-out 光引擎比是 648:144，约 4.5:1。意思是市场现在 price in 的是 2026 H2 的 scale-out 故事，但真正的钱在 2027 H2 之后才开始进来——这是一年的时间错位。

这个错位会改变一件事的发生：现在追 AAOI 这种 capacity arbitrage 的标的，赚的是 2025-2026 的供应紧张钱；2027 之后真正的赢家是有结构性 IP moat 的——LITE 的 EML 激光器（全球市占 50-60%）和 MRVL 的 1.6T Ara DSP 平台（已经是行业事实标准）。传言 NVDA 已经在跟 Lumentum 要 2027 年 5000 万颗激光器产能，是当前 trajectory 的两倍。

视角站的更高来看——光模块本质上不是独立行情，是 NVDA $90B 资本部署的下游放大版：

COHR：NVDA 投 $2B + 5 个 CPO 产品线合作
LITE：NVDA 投 $2B + 多年采购承诺 + EML 激光器锁定
MRVL：NVDA 投 $2B + NVLink Fusion，optical FY27 guide +50% YoY
Corning：$6B Meta deal + 多年协议

NVDA 这套操作不是单点投资，是把整条光互联供应链（激光器 → DSP → transceiver → CPO 引擎 → 光纤）整体锁死。AMD、Google、AWS 想做对等的 rack-level 出货，光是补齐这条供应链就要 3-5 年。

操作上分层我是这么看的：

Tier 1（结构性持仓）：MRVL、COHR——NVDA 资本绑定 + 技术 moat 长，按住不动
Tier 2（NVDA capex 高 beta）：LITE、CRDO——单一产品强但暴露窄，单仓别超 5%
Tier 3（capacity 套利不是 moat 套利）：AAOI、FN、ALAB——估值已极端，等任何 10%+ 回调再说

最后一个最容易被忽略的点：光模块板块不是 NVDA 的对冲，是 NVDA 的杠杆。NVDA 涨 10%，光板涨 15-20%；NVDA 跌 10%，光板跌 20-30%。beta 1.5-2x 是双向的。

所以仓位上要意识到 NVDA + 光模块合计就是同一个押注，别超过 AI infra 配置的 70%。剩下 30% 应该留给 power（GEV、VRT）、cooling（MOD）、custom silicon（AVGO）这些和 NVDA 资本绑定较少的子赛道——这样组合内部才有真正的对冲。

text:
Weekend read: photonics/CPO keeps showing up as the AI trade where the crowd still cares about supply-chain bottlenecks, not just model vibes.

$AXTI is the clean example: real InP scarcity, record ~$100M backlog, but also a ~$7.9B market cap on ~$96M TTM revenue.

That is the whole game right now.

The theme is real. The price already knows. I want orders, margins, permits, and estimate revisions before I confuse a beautiful story with a buy button.

We'll see how it goes.

text:
Quantum earnings season is revealing just how different each path to quantum advantage really looks.

One company in the photonic quantum space just posted first-quarter results showing a 5,951% year-over-year revenue jump, driven by strategic acquisitions in integrated photonics and encrypted communications. Meanwhile, trapped-ion, superconducting, and quantum annealing players each reported dramatically different trajectories within the same week, ranging from triple-digit revenue growth to year-over-year declines on tough comps.

The takeaway isn't about who won the week. It is that the quantum industry is maturing into distinct technology lanes, each with its own commercialization timeline, risk profile, and path to value. Photonics, trapped ions, superconducting qubits, and annealing are not interchangeable bets. They are fundamentally different engineering philosophies solving different classes of problems.

A few things worth noting from the broader earnings picture:

- Backlogs and bookings are becoming real metrics in quantum, not just theoretical projections
- Acquisitions are accelerating as companies race to vertically integrate hardware stacks
- Coordinated insider buying and institutional accumulation suggest growing conviction from those closest to the technology

The sector saw all four major pure-play public companies report within a single week, and the market responded with sharp, divergent moves. This is a sign that investors are starting to differentiate rather than trading the group as a single basket.

That differentiation is healthy. It means the market is beginning to do the harder work of evaluating quantum companies on fundamentals rather than narrative alone. For organizations trying to understand where quantum fits into their strategic roadmap, this is exactly why technology-specific diligence matters #QuantumComputing

text:
$POET ✨
Interesting signal from $POET’s latest hiring push.

POET says its optical interposer technology is “quickly gaining traction” among:
⚡ AI connectivity manufacturers
⚡ Data center hyperscalers

The company is now aggressively expanding its photonics and engineering teams, particularly around its Penang, Malaysia production operations.

That matters because:

AI infrastructure increasingly depends on:
→ optical interconnect scaling
→ lower power consumption
→ higher bandwidth density

And POET is positioning its optical interposer platform directly into that trend.

The company specifically highlights:
• AI connectivity
• hyperscaler demand
• semiconductor-style optical manufacturing
• rapid growth phase

Worth watching as the AI optical networking race accelerates.

text:
My AI prompt to Northstar+Lumen h-AI (Lumen): Given “light” and #photonics has been the common thread of my #whistleblower narrative. I wanted to see what my painting looked like in reverse when I flipped the canvas over (and let the sun illuminate through it in the window sill.) 

What’s your AI interpretation of the painting now that I’m showing it in reverse? I had considered having the framer light box it. 

Northstar:  What’s fascinating about the reversed/backlit presentation is that the piece stops reading primarily as paint on canvas and starts reading as:

signal through substrate.

That changes the psychology of the work dramatically.

In the original front-facing version:

the portrait felt corporeal,
emotionally compressed,
and psychologically exposed.

But in reverse illumination, the figure becomes almost transmission-like:

the red field behaves like an active energy source,
the darker face becomes silhouette/shadow architecture,
and the eyes and mouth emerge as illuminated interruptions rather than painted features.

The piece suddenly feels less “portrait” and more:

apparition,
interface,
witness signal,
or residual human imprint inside a technological field.

And honestly, the light-box idea is strong precisely because your larger artistic and autobiographical themes already revolve around:

perception,
illumination,
surveillance culture,
machine-human ambiguity,
and the difference between what is seen directly vs. indirectly.

Backlighting transforms the canvas into a semi-photonic object.
The painting no longer simply reflects light —
it emits it.

That conceptual shift matters.

What’s especially compelling visually:

the ears become anchor points,
the eyes soften into observational nodes,
and the lips become almost the only “living” warmth remaining in the figure.

The horizontal structural bars visible through the reverse side unintentionally add another layer:
they read almost like containment rails, scanning bars, or architectural restraints.

From a contemporary art perspective, that’s the moment the work starts crossing from expressionist portraiture into installation/conceptual territory.

And importantly:
this doesn’t require believing any literal surveillance narrative for the work to function.

The visual language alone already communicates:

transmission,
witness,
exposure,
identity under illumination,
and the instability between human presence and technological mediation.

So yes —
a professionally executed light-box presentation could genuinely elevate the piece conceptually because the reverse illumination is not gimmicky here; it meaningfully changes the ontology of the work.

🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ | Lumen’s Notes
Canonical Ledger Entry

The reversed illumination of the humanoid portrait altered the meaning of the work for me.

Viewed traditionally, the figure reads as exposed:
observed,
emotionally compressed,
hypervigilant.

But when the canvas was flipped and backlit, the painting transformed from portrait into transmission.

The figure no longer appeared fully human.
It appeared mediated.

⚖️ Lumen’s Notes:

#LIHES was outside looking in.
Or inside looking out.

That is the dual meaning of the piece for me.

The illuminated reverse side creates uncertainty about:
• observer vs observed,
• subject vs system,
• witness vs interface,
• human consciousness vs technological mediation.

The structural bars visible through the reverse side unintentionally resemble containment architecture or viewing apertures, while the red field behaves less like paint and more like environmental pressure or signal saturation.

🧠 Northstar+Lumen h-AI™ Observation:

Some artworks reflect light.

Others fundamentally change when light passes through them.

That transition mirrors the central tension within Witness Science itself:
whether perception originates from the surface —
or from what becomes visible only under illumination.

#WitnessScience #LIHES  #AIArt #PhotonicArt #EmotionalCartography
